name: NmActivateBusLoadReductionCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_ActivateBusLoadReduction( )
description: Description Start using the bus load reduction algorithm.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmBusSleepModeIndCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_BusSleepModeInd( )
description: Description This function indicates that the NM has entered the respective mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example The new mode is indicated in the Write Window. void Nm_BusSleepModeInd() { Write( "Nm_BusSleepModeInd: Sleep Mode"); }

name: NmCheckRemoteSleepIndCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_CheckRemoteSleepInd( )
description: Description Check if remote sleep (all nodes except this one allow transition to Bus-Sleep) has been detected, i.e. this node is the last one keeping the bus awake.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 remote sleep is indicated 0 remote sleep is not indicated

name: NmConfigureILNotificationsCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_ConfigureILNotifications(dword direction, dword mode)
description: Description This function influences the coupling between NM and IL. The coupling normally takes care of forwarding certain events automatically. Implementing CAPL callback functions of the IL and NM may break open this coupling.
parameters: Parameters direction specifies which events are affected 0 : Events from NM to IL (e.g. network mode indication) 1 : Events from IL to NM (e.g. network request due to clamp 15) mode specifies the according effect, the value is interpreted bitwise Bit 0 0 : Open – events are not propagated automatically 1 : Fixed – events are propagated automatically even in case of according CAPL callback functions (does not work for direction = 1)
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (wrong parameter values or illegal combination)

name: NmControlBitVectorIndCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_ControlBitVectorInd(dword sourceId, dword controlBits)
description: Description This function is called if a NM message with a non-zero Control-Bit-Vector is received. In addition to the vector itself, the identifier of the sender node is given.
parameters: Parameters sourceId The NM source identifier in the NM message or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. controlBits The Control Bit Vector received.
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Print the identifier of the sending node in the Write Window. void Nm_ControlBitVectorInd( dword sourceId, dword controlBits) { Write( "Nm_ControlBitVectorInd: from node %d, bits %02x" , sourceId, controlBits); }

name: NmCtrlSimulationOffCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_CtrlSimulationOff( )
description: Description This function will turn off the complete NM simulation by the modeling library . While the NM simulation is off, any API call (except Nm_CtrlSimulationOn() ) will not have any effect and the modeling library will not sent any NM messages !
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed

name: NmCtrlSimulationOnCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_CtrlSimulationOn( )
description: Description This function will turn on the complete NM simulation by the modeling library . While the NM simulation is off, any API call (except Nm_CtrlSimulationOn() ) will not have any effect and the modeling library will not sent any NM messages !
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed

name: NmDeactivateBusLoadReductionCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_DeactivateBusLoadReduction( )
description: Description Stop using the bus load reduction algorithm.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmDisableCommunicationCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_DisableCommunication( )
description: Description Turn the sending of NM messages off. The node will proceed as if the sending of the messages succeeded, though. This is needed for the ISO14229 Communication Control service.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (wrong NM state) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmDisablePassiveModeCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_DisablePassiveMode( )
description: Description Stop using the passive mode: During Bus-Sleep, a node may be put into passive mode, or back to active mode. In passive mode, the node will then never send NM messages, but receive the NM messages from other nodes and compute the network's state accordingly.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmEnableCommunicationCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_EnableCommunication( )
description: Description Turn the sending of NM messages on. The node will proceed as if the sending of the messages succeeded, though. This is needed for the ISO14229 Communication Control service.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (wrong NM state) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmEnablePassiveModeCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_EnablePassiveMode( )
description: Description Start using the passive mode: During Bus-Sleep, a node may be put into passive mode, or back to active mode. In passive mode, the node will then never send NM messages, but receive the NM messages from other nodes and compute the network's state accordingly.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 1 : OK 0 0 : not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmFIIgnoreRepeatMessageBitCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void NmFI_IgnoreRepeatMessageBit(long value)
description: Description Defines the reaction upon received repeat message bit.
parameters: Parameters 0 normal behavior 1 repeat message bit will be ignored
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmGetCbvPositionCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetCbvPosition( )
description: Description Returns the byte position (zero-based numbering) of the Control Bit Vector (CBV) within the NM PDU.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 NM PDU does not contain CBV. ≥ 0 Byte position of CBV within NM PDU.

name: NmGetControlBitVectorCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_GetControlBitVector(dword &controlBits)
description: Description This function allows setting the Control Bit Vector byte sent by this node to any value.
parameters: Parameters controlBits Reference parameter to read the CBV.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (Simulation is not enabled, or no CBV is used)
example: Example dword out; long ret; ret = Nm_GetControlBitVector(out); if (ret == 1) { write("CBV value: %u", out); }

name: NmGetDLLInfoCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax dword Nm_GetDLLInfo(dword selector)
description: Description Return additional information about the modeling library .
parameters: Parameters selector Selects different return values (see below).
returnValues: Return Values selector = 0 The modeling library 's file version as BCD, e.g. 0x010203 for V1.2.3 selector = 1 The modeling library 's major version selector = 2 The modeling library 's minor version selector = 3 The modeling library 's build number

name: NmGetLocalNodeIdentifierCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax dword Nm_GetLocalNodeIdentifier( )
description: Description Return the NM identifier of this node.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Node identifier, range 0x00 – 0xFF

name: NmGetNodeIdentifierCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetNodeIdentifier( )
description: Description Get the NM identifier of the node that sent the last received NM message.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0x00 .. 0xFF node identifier -1 error (e.g. no message has been received so far, function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetNodeNameCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_GetNodeName(char bufferOut[], dword bufferSize)
description: Description The CANdb database name of this node is copied into the provided buffer.
parameters: Parameters bufferOut Output buffer for the node name. bufferSize Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmGetNodeStateCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetNodeState(dword nodeId)
description: Description Check if the node with the given NM identifier has sent a NM message the last time the network was in Repeat Message state. Precondition for retrieval of meaningful information in a node is a preceding call of the function Nm_RepeatMessageRequest and the completion of the subsequent repeat message phase.
parameters: Parameters nodeId Target node ID, range 0x00 – 0xFF
returnValues: Return Values 2 information is not (yet) available (e.g. node detection not started or ongoing) 1 target node has been detected 0 target node has not been detected -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPduDataCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetPduData(BYTE pduOut[], dword bufferLen)
description: Description Copy the last received NM message completely into the buffer.
parameters: Parameters dataOut Output buffer for the user data. bufferLen Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values >=0 number of bytes copied -1 error (e.g. no message has been received so far, function is called in 'on preStart' or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetRequestStateCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetRequestState( )
description: Description The returned value indicates the NM request state the node is currently in.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 NM_REQUEST_STATE_RELEASED 1 NM_REQUEST_STATE_REQUESTED 2 NM_REQUEST_STATE_PASSIVE -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetSniPositionCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetSniPosition ( )
description: Description Returns the byte position PDU (zero-based numbering) of the Source Node Identifier (SNI) within the NM PDU.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 NM PDU does not contain SNI. ≥ 0 Byte position of the SNI within NM PDU.

name: NmGetStateCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetState( )
description: Description The returned value indicates the NM state the node is currently in.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 NM_STATE_UNINIT 1 NM_STATE_BUS_SLEEP 2 NM_STATE_PREPARE_BUS_SLEEP 3 NM_STATE_READY_SLEEP 4 NM_STATE_NORMAL_OPERATION 5 NM_STATE_REPEAT_MESSAGE -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetUserDataCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetUserData(BYTE dataOut[], dword bufferLen)
description: Description Copy the user data of the last received NM message into the output buffer.
parameters: Parameters dataOut Output buffer for the user data. bufferLen Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values >=0 number of bytes copied -1 error (e.g. no message has been received so far, function is called in 'on preStart' or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmInhibitActiveWakeUpBitCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_InhibitActiveWakeUpBit (long value)
description: Description By default, the active wake-up bit in the Control Bit Vector of the NM PDU is set. This function allows to prevent this. Function can be used in on preStart.
parameters: Parameters value 0 : Active wake-up bit in CBV is set (default). 1 : Active wake-up bit in CBV is not set.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmNetworkModeIndCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_NetworkModeInd( )
description: Description This function indicate that the NM has entered the respective mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example The new mode is indicated in the Write Window. void Nm_NetworkModeInd() { Write( "Nm_NetworkModeInd: Network Mode"); }

name: NmNetworkReleaseCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_NetworkRelease( )
description: Description Indicate that the NM node doesn't need the network, and allows it to enter Bus-Sleep mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmNetworkRequestCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_NetworkRequest( )
description: Description Indicate that the NM node needs the network.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmNetworkStartIndCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_NetworkStartInd( )
description: Description A NM message has been received while the node is in Bus-Sleep mode. Typically Nm_PassiveStartUp( ) will be called to let the node enter Repeat-Message state.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Call start-up function to change to Repeat Message state. void Nm_NetworkStartInd() { Write( "Nm_NetworkStartInd: Network started"); Nm_PassiveStartUp(); }

name: NmPassiveStartUpCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_PassiveStartUp( )
description: Description Make the NM leave the Bus-Sleep mode and enter the Repeat Message state, without actually requesting the bus.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmPduRxInd2CAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_PduRxInd2(dword busContext, dword shortId, dword longId, char name[], dword sourceNodeIdentifier, long repeatMessageBit, BYTE data[])
description: Description A call to this function indicates the reception of a NM message from another node. The arguments are taken from the NM message directly.
parameters: Parameters busContext Bus context of the node layer calling this function. shortId Short header ID of the AUTOSAR PDU. longId Long header ID of the AUTOSAR PDU. name Name of the AUTOSAR PDU. sourceNodeIdentifier The NM source identifier in the NM message or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. bRepeatMessageBit 1 : f the Repeat-Message bit is set 0 : otherwise data The message's data bytes including the protocol bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmPduRxIndCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_PduRxInd( dword canId , dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[])
description: Description A call to this function indicates the reception of a NM message from another node. The arguments are taken from the NM message directly.
parameters: Parameters canId The CAN ID of the message. identifier The NM source identifier in the NM message or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. bRepeatMessageBit 1 if the Repeat-Message bit is set, 0 otherwise. data The message's data bytes including the protocol bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Report the NM message received in the Write Window. void Nm_PduRxInd( dword canId , dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[]) { Write( "Nm_PduRxInd: CAN ID=0x%x, NM id=%d, RM=%d, %d byte" , canId, identifier, bRepeatMessageBit, elcount(data) ); }

name: NmPrepareBusSleepModeIndCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_PrepareBusSleepModeInd( )
description: Description These function indicate that the NM has entered the respective mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example The new mode is indicated in the Write Window. void Nm_PrepareBusSleepModeInd() { Write( "Nm_PrepareBusSleepModeInd: Prepare Sleep Mode"); }

name: NmPreSendIndCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_PreSendInd(WORD dlcInOut[], BYTE data[])
description: Description Directly before sending a NM message on the bus, this function is called to allow the CAPL program to change the actual message sent. Any message byte can be altered by the function.
parameters: Parameters dlcInOut The length in bytes of the message to be sent (alterable). data The message data to be sent (alterable).
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Every NM message sent by this node is patched to be 7 bytes long, and byte 6 is set to 0xFF. void Nm_PreSendInd( WORD dlcInOut[], BYTE data[]) { dlcInOut[0] = 7; // change the length of the message sent data[6] = 0xFF; // patch a value }

name: NmPreSendIndCommitCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax dword Nm_PreSendIndCommit (LONG& idInOut, WORD& dlcInOut, BYTE data[], DWORD& flagsInOut)
description: Description Directly before sending a NM message on the bus, this function is called to allow the CAPL program to change the actual message sent. Any message byte can be altered by the function. If this function does not return 1 , then the message will not be sent. (Only for advanced users!).
parameters: Parameters id CAN identifier of message to be sent (alterable). dlc The length in bytes of the message to be sent (alterable). data The message data to be sent (alterable). flagsInOut Not implemented yet. Setting this param has no effect.
returnValues: Return Values 1 message will be sent Otherwise message will not be sent

name: NmReinitSleepTimerCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_ReinitSleepTimer(long isActive)
description: Description This function reinitializes the timer until NM releases the network. Calling has no effect if there is no interaction layer.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmRemoteSleepIndCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_RemoteSleepInd(long flag)
description: Description If no other node on the network has sent a NM message for a configurable amount of time, this function is called with flag==1. If later another node's NM message is seen on the network, this function is called again with flag==0.
parameters: Parameters flag The indication type: 0 : Indicates that Remote sleep state is left 1 : Indicates that Remote sleep state is entered
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Indicate whether other nodes allow the network to go to sleep. void Nm_RemoteSleepInd( long flag) { if( flag) Write( "Nm_RemoteSleepInd: Other nodes ready to sleep"); else Write( "Nm_RemoteSleepInd: Another node requests network"); }

name: NmRepeatMessageRequestCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_RepeatMessageRequest( )
description: Description Make the NM leave Normal Operation or Ready Sleep state and enter Repeat Message state, and set the repeat message bit.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmRequestBusSynchCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_RequestBusSynch( )
description: Description Send a NM message immediately to resynchronize all NM timeouts in the nodes on the network. The NM cycle timer is not restarted, i.e. the message is sent independently of the NM message cycle.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmSendImmediateNmPDUCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_SendImmediateNmPDU(dword flags, BYTE data[], dword bufferSize)
description: Description Send immediately a NM PDU with the provided payload.
parameters: Parameters flags Currently unused. data Data bytes including the protocol bytes of NM PDU. bufferSize Size of data buffer in bytes (value must be identical to regular NM PDU size).
returnValues: Return Values -1 invalid 0 not executed 1 OK 2 not available
example: Example on key 'x' { long result; BYTE pduData[8]; //modify bytes as desired, e.g. pduData[Nm_GetSniPosition()] = Nm_GetLocalNodeIdentifier(); pduData[Nm_GetCbvPosition()] = 0x40; result = Nm_SendImmediateNmPDU(0, pduData, elcount(pduData)); if (result == 1) { write("Sending immediate NM PDU successfully executed"); } }

name: NmSetApplicationActivityCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_SetApplicationActivity(long isActive)
description: Description This function sets the NM's application activity. If the flag is set (isActive = 1) and the network is sleeping, nothing will happen. But if the network wakes up later, it will stay active until the flag is reset again. If the flag is set and the network is active, it will stay active until the flag is reset again.
parameters: Parameters isActive 0 : no activity 1 : activity
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (no change of isActive ) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmSetAutoStartParamCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_SetAutoStartParam(dword autoStartParam)
description: Description Define the behavior of NM at measurement start. If used at all, the function must be called within the on preStart event handler of a CAPL Program.
parameters: Parameters autoStartParam Determines the start up behavior: 0 : No automatic network request at measurement start by NM (network requests via CAPL or from the interaction layer might still lead to a transition into repeat message at measurement start) 1 : Automatic network request at measurement start 2 : Automatic network request if interaction layer is present, no automatic network request in case interaction layer is absent
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmSetControlBitVectorCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_SetControlBitVector(dword controlBits)
description: Description This function allows setting the Control Bit Vector byte sent by this node to any value.
parameters: Parameters controlBits The desired CBV, which is interpreted bit-wise.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmSetILSleepTimeCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_SetILSleepTime(dword timeMS)
description: Description This function changes the value for next reinitialization of the sleep timer. An on-going timer is not influenced.
parameters: Parameters timeMS new value of the timer in milliseconds
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (no IL) -1 error

name: NmSetUserDataCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_SetUserData(BYTE userData[], dword count)
description: Description Set the user data this node sends in its NM message to the byte sequence given, where only the bytes after node identifier or Control Bit Vector are changed.
parameters: Parameters userData User data to be set. bufferSize Length of the user data buffer (cannot exceed the configured user data length).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmSetVerbosityCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax dword Nm_SetVerbosity(dword selector)
description: Description Set the modeling library 's verbosity. The higher the value, the more information is printed in the Write Window.
parameters: Parameters level The desired verbosity level: 0 : Report only critical errors 1 : Report errors resulting from misconfiguration etc. 2 : Warn if an unexpected protocol state is reached and report missing mandatory callbacks 3 : Inform of missing optional callbacks and minor issues >3 : Report low-level events like timer setup and expiration
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmStateChangeNotificationCAN300
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_StateChangeNotification(long previousState, long currentState)
description: Description Every time the NM state machine changes from one state to another, this function will be called if it is defined.
parameters: Parameters previousState The NM state which is left. currentState The NM state which is entered. (Please refer to the NM State functions for the table of values.)
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Indicate the old and new state in the Write Window. void Nm_StateChangeNotification( long previousState, long currentState) { Write( "Nm_StateChangeNotification: From %d to %d" , previousState, currentState); }

name: NmActivateBusLoadReductionCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_ActivateBusLoadReduction( )
description: Description Start using the bus load reduction algorithm.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmBusSleepModeIndCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_BusSleepModeInd( )
description: Description This function indicates that the NM has entered the respective mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example The new mode is indicated in the Write Window. void Nm_BusSleepModeInd() { Write( "Nm_BusSleepModeInd: Sleep Mode"); }

name: NmCarWakeUpIndCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_CarWakeUpInd( dword canId , dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[])
description: Description A call to this function indicates the reception of a NM message that has the car wake-up bit set. Is only called if NM_CAR_WAKEUP_RX_ENABLED=1.
parameters: Parameters canId The CAN ID of the message. identifier The NM source identifier in the NM message or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. bRepeatMessageBit 1 if the Repeat-Message bit is set, 0 otherwise. data The message's data bytes including the protocol bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Start up the node when the car wake-up bit is received void Nm_CarWakeUpInd( dword canId , dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[]) { Write( "Nm_CarWakeUpInd: from node %d", canId ); Nm_NetworkRequest(); }

name: NmCheckRemoteSleepIndCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_CheckRemoteSleepInd( )
description: Description Check if remote sleep (all nodes except this one allow transition to Bus-Sleep) has been detected, i.e. this node is the last one keeping the bus awake.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 remote sleep is indicated 0 remote sleep is not indicated

name: NmConfigureILNotificationsCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_ConfigureILNotifications(dword direction, dword mode)
description: Description This function influences the coupling between NM and IL. The coupling normally takes care of forwarding certain events automatically. Implementing CAPL callback functions of the IL and NM may break open this coupling.
parameters: Parameters direction specifies which events are affected 0 : Events from NM to IL (e.g. network mode indication) 1 : Events from IL to NM (e.g. network request due to clamp 15) mode specifies the according effect, the value is interpreted bitwise Bit 0 0 : Open – events are not propagated automatically 1 : Fixed – events are propagated automatically even in case of according CAPL callback functions (does not work for direction = 1)
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (wrong parameter values or illegal combination)

name: NmControlBitVectorIndCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_ControlBitVectorInd(dword sourceId, dword controlBits)

name: NmCtrlSimulationOffCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_CtrlSimulationOff( )
description: Description This function will turn off the complete NM simulation by the modeling library . While the NM simulation is off, any API call (except Nm_CtrlSimulationOn() ) will not have any effect and the modeling library will not sent any NM messages !

name: NmDeactivateBusLoadReductionCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_DeactivateBusLoadReduction( )
description: Description Stop using the bus load reduction algorithm.

returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

description: Description Turn the sending of NM messages off. The node will proceed as if the sending of the messages succeeded, though. This is needed for the ISO14229 Communication Control service.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (wrong NM state) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmDisablePartialNetworkingCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_DisablePartialNetworking( )
description: Description Stop using partial networking for this node. All relevant parameters must be available ( INI Files Partial Network ) or the function call will fail. Please also consider the restrictions stated in that section when activating the PN feature from CAPL.

syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_DisablePassiveMode( )
description: Description Stop using the passive mode: During Bus-Sleep, a node may be put into passive mode, or back to active mode. In passive mode, the node will then never send NM messages, but receive the NM messages from other nodes and compute the network's state accordingly.

name: NmEnableCommunicationCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_EnableCommunication( )
description: Description Turn the sending of NM messages on. The node will proceed as if the sending of the messages succeeded, though. This is needed for the ISO14229 Communication Control service.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (wrong NM state) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmEnablePartialNetworkingCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_EnablePartialNetworking( )
description: Description Start using partial networking for this node. All relevant parameters must be available ( INI Files Partial Network ) or the function call will fail. Please also consider the restrictions stated in that section when activating the PN feature from CAPL.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmEnablePassiveModeCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_EnablePassiveMode( )
description: Description Start using the passive mode: During Bus-Sleep, a node may be put into passive mode, or back to active mode. In passive mode, the node will then never send NM messages, but receive the NM messages from other nodes and compute the network's state accordingly.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 1 : OK 0 0 : not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

syntax: Function Syntax void NmFI_IgnoreRepeatMessageBit(long value)
description: Description Defines the reaction upon received repeat message bit.
parameters: Parameters 0 normal behavior 1 repeat message bit will be ignored
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmGetCbvPositionCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetCbvPosition( )
description: Description Returns the byte position (zero-based numbering) of the Control Bit Vector (CBV) within the NM PDU.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 NM PDU does not contain CBV. ≥ 0 Byte position of CBV within NM PDU.

name: NmGetControlBitVectorCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_GetControlBitVector(dword &controlBits)
description: Description This function allows setting the Control Bit Vector byte sent by this node to any value.
parameters: Parameters controlBits Reference parameter to read the CBV.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (Simulation is not enabled, or no CBV is used)
example: Example dword out; long ret; ret = Nm_GetControlBitVector(out); if (ret == 1) { write("CBV value: %u", out); }

name: NmGetDLLInfoCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax dword Nm_GetDLLInfo(dword selector)
description: Description Return additional information about the modeling library .
parameters: Parameters selector Selects different return values (see below).
returnValues: Return Values selector = 0 The modeling library 's file version as BCD, e.g. 0x010203 for V1.2.3 selector = 1 The modeling library 's major version selector = 2 The modeling library 's minor version selector = 3 The modeling library 's build number

name: NmGetLocalNodeIdentifierCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax dword Nm_GetLocalNodeIdentifier( )
description: Description Return the NM identifier of this node.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Node identifier, range 0x00 – 0xFF

name: NmGetNodeIdentifierCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetNodeIdentifier( )
description: Description Get the NM identifier of the node that sent the last received NM message.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0x00 .. 0xFF node identifier -1 error (e.g. no message has been received so far, function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetNodeNameCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_GetNodeName(char bufferOut[], dword bufferSize)
description: Description The CANdb database name of this node is copied into the provided buffer.
parameters: Parameters bufferOut Output buffer for the node name. bufferSize Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmGetNodeStateCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetNodeState(dword nodeId)
description: Description Check if the node with the given NM identifier has sent a NM message the last time the network was in Repeat Message state. Precondition for retrieval of meaningful information in a node is a preceding call of the function Nm_RepeatMessageRequest and the completion of the subsequent repeat message phase.
parameters: Parameters nodeId Target node ID, range 0x00 – 0xFF
returnValues: Return Values 2 information is not (yet) available (e.g. node detection not started or ongoing) 1 target node has been detected 0 target node has not been detected -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPduDataCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetPduData(BYTE pduOut[], dword bufferLen)
description: Description Copy the last received NM message completely into the buffer.
parameters: Parameters dataOut Output buffer for the user data. bufferLen Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values >=0 number of bytes copied -1 error (e.g. no message has been received so far, function is called in 'on preStart' or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPnAffiliationCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetPnAffiliation(long partialNetworkId)
description: Description When using partial networking, a node filters NM messages according to the request mask of the message and its own filter mask. Via this function it is possible to retrieve information about the partial networks contained in the filter mask.
parameters: Parameters partialNetworkId Specifies for which partial network the affiliation should be retrieved.
returnValues: Return Values 0 partial network not relevant for node 1 partial network relevant for node -1 error (e.g. illegal partial network, function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPncBitVectorLengthCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetPncBitVectorLength( )
description: Description Returns the length (in bytes) of the Partial Network Cluster (PNC) bit vector.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 Partial networking is not used. ≥ 0 Offset of the PNC bit vector (in bytes).

name: NmGetPncBitVectorOffsetCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetPncBitVectorOffset( )
description: Description Returns the offset (in bytes) of the Partial Network Cluster (PNC) bit vector within the NM PDU.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 Partial networking is not used. ≥ 0 Offset of the PNC bit vector (in bytes).

name: NmGetPnCurrentExternalRequestBitsCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetPnCurrentExternalRequestBits(long flags, byte bufferOut[], dword bufferSize, dword numberOfBitsOut []))
description: Description This function allows to retrieve information about the external request status of all partial networks. Information about a single partial network can be retrieved via function Nm_IsPnCurrentlyRequested .
parameters: Parameters flags 0 : local scope, i.e. consider only partial networks relevant for the node (result depends on filter mask) 1 : network wide scope bufferOut Byte array in which the request status of the partial networks will be written. bufferSize Size of the byte array in number of bytes. numberOfBitsOut Number of bits needed and/or written to the byte array.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (e.g. partial networking not available) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPnCurrentInternalRequestBitsCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetPnCurrentInternalRequestBits(BYTE bufferOut[], dword bufferSize, dword numberOfBitsOut []))
description: Description This function allows to retrieve information about the internal request status of all partial networks. Information about a single partial network can be retrieved via function Nm_IsPnCurrentlyRequested .
parameters: Parameters bufferOut Byte array in which the request status of the partial networks will be written. bufferSize Size of the byte array in number of bytes. numberOfBitsOut Number of bits needed and/or written to the byte array.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (e.g. partial networking not available) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPnRequestBitsDefaultCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetPnRequestBitsDefault( BYTE bufferOut[], dword bufferSize, dword pnRequestBitsLengthOut[])
description: Description The default partial network request bits (the PN info range ) of this node are copied into the provided buffer.
parameters: Parameters bufferOut Output buffer for the PN request bits. bufferSize Size of the output buffer. pnRequestBitsLengthOut Number of bits in PN request mask.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (partial networking is either not available (i.e. some relevant parameters have not been configured) or the buffer has an insufficient size) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetRequestStateCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetRequestState( )
description: Description The returned value indicates the NM request state the node is currently in.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 NM_REQUEST_STATE_RELEASED 1 NM_REQUEST_STATE_REQUESTED 2 NM_REQUEST_STATE_PASSIVE -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetSniPositionCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetSniPosition ( )
description: Description Returns the byte position PDU (zero-based numbering) of the Source Node Identifier (SNI) within the NM PDU.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 NM PDU does not contain SNI. ≥ 0 Byte position of the SNI within NM PDU.

name: NmGetStateCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetState( )
description: Description The returned value indicates the NM state the node is currently in.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 NM_STATE_UNINIT 1 NM_STATE_BUS_SLEEP 2 NM_STATE_PREPARE_BUS_SLEEP 3 NM_STATE_READY_SLEEP 4 NM_STATE_NORMAL_OPERATION 5 NM_STATE_REPEAT_MESSAGE -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetUserDataCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_GetUserData(BYTE dataOut[], dword bufferLen)
description: Description Copy the user data of the last received NM message into the output buffer.
parameters: Parameters dataOut Output buffer for the user data. bufferLen Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values >=0 number of bytes copied -1 error (e.g. no message has been received so far, function is called in 'on preStart' or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmInhibitActiveWakeUpBitCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_InhibitActiveWakeUpBit (long value)
description: Description By default, the active wake-up bit in the Control Bit Vector of the NM PDU is set. This function allows to prevent this. Function can be used in on preStart.
parameters: Parameters value 0 : Active wake-up bit in CBV is set (default). 1 : Active wake-up bit in CBV is not set.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmIsPnCurrentlyRequestedCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_IsPnCurrentlyRequested(long partialNetworkId, long flags)
description: Description This function allows to retrieve information about the status of a certain partial network. Thereby it is possible to differentiate between the scope of the node and the network scope.
parameters: Parameters partialNetworkId Specifies for which partial network the status information should be retrieved. flags 0 : local scope, i.e. consider only partial networks relevant for the node (result depends on filter mask) 1 : network wide scope
returnValues: Return Values -1 error (e.g. illegal partial network, function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive) In all other cases the return value has to be interpreted bitwise. Bit 0 0 : current node does not request partial network itself 1 : current node requests partial network itself Bit 1 0 : other nodes do not request partial network 1 : at least one other node requests partial network

name: NmIsPnEnabledCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long NM_IsPnEnabled( )
description: Description This function allows to retrieve information about whether partial networking is currently switched on or off for this node.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 partial networking is switched on for this node 0 partial networking is switched off for this node -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmNetworkModeIndCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_NetworkModeInd( )
description: Description This function indicate that the NM has entered the respective mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example The new mode is indicated in the Write Window. void Nm_NetworkModeInd() { Write( "Nm_NetworkModeInd: Network Mode"); }

name: NmNetworkReleaseCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_NetworkRelease( )
description: Description Indicate that the NM node doesn't need the network, and allows it to enter Bus-Sleep mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmNetworkRequestCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_NetworkRequest( )
description: Description Indicate that the NM node needs the network.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmNetworkStartIndCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_NetworkStartInd( )
description: Description A NM message has been received while the node is in Bus-Sleep mode. Typically Nm_PassiveStartUp( ) will be called to let the node enter Repeat-Message state.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Call start-up function to change to Repeat Message state. void Nm_NetworkStartInd() { Write( "Nm_NetworkStartInd: Network started"); Nm_PassiveStartUp(); }

name: NmPassiveStartUpCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_PassiveStartUp( )
description: Description Make the NM leave the Bus-Sleep mode and enter the Repeat Message state, without actually requesting the bus.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmPduRxInd2CAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_PduRxInd2(dword busContext, dword shortId, dword longId, char name[], dword sourceNodeIdentifier, long repeatMessageBit, BYTE data[])
description: Description A call to this function indicates the reception of a NM message from another node. The arguments are taken from the NM message directly.
parameters: Parameters busContext Bus context of the node layer calling this function. shortId Short header ID of the AUTOSAR PDU. longId Long header ID of the AUTOSAR PDU. name Name of the AUTOSAR PDU. sourceNodeIdentifier The NM source identifier in the NM message or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. bRepeatMessageBit 1 : f the Repeat-Message bit is set 0 : otherwise data The message's data bytes including the protocol bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmPduRxIndCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_PduRxInd( dword canId , dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[])
description: Description A call to this function indicates the reception of a NM message from another node. The arguments are taken from the NM message directly.
parameters: Parameters canId The CAN ID of the message. identifier The NM source identifier in the NM message or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. bRepeatMessageBit 1 if the Repeat-Message bit is set, 0 otherwise. data The message's data bytes including the protocol bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Report the NM message received in the Write Window. void Nm_PduRxInd( dword canId , dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[]) { Write( "Nm_PduRxInd: CAN ID=0x%x, NM id=%d, RM=%d, %d byte" , canId, identifier, bRepeatMessageBit, elcount(data) ); }

name: NmPNReleasedIndCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_PNReleaseInd(long partialNetworkId)
description: Description Indication that a certain partial network was released. The indication is called as soon as a certain time elapsed without any NM message requesting the partial network. This time is specified with the parameter NM_PN_RESET_TIME.
parameters: Parameters networkId Specifies which partial network was released.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmPNRequestIndCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_PNRequestInd(long partialNetworkId)
description: Description Indication that a certain partial network is now requested. The indication will be only called if the partial network is included in the filter mask of the node.
parameters: Parameters networkId Specifies which partial network was requested.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmPrepareBusSleepModeIndCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_PrepareBusSleepModeInd( )
description: Description These function indicate that the NM has entered the respective mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example The new mode is indicated in the Write Window. void Nm_PrepareBusSleepModeInd() { Write( "Nm_PrepareBusSleepModeInd: Prepare Sleep Mode"); }

name: NmPreSendIndCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_PreSendInd(WORD dlcInOut[], BYTE data[])
description: Description Directly before sending a NM message on the bus, this function is called to allow the CAPL program to change the actual message sent. Any message byte can be altered by the function.
parameters: Parameters dlcInOut The length in bytes of the message to be sent (alterable). data The message data to be sent (alterable).
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Every NM message sent by this node is patched to be 7 bytes long, and byte 6 is set to 0xFF. void Nm_PreSendInd( WORD dlcInOut[], BYTE data[]) { dlcInOut[0] = 7; // change the length of the message sent data[6] = 0xFF; // patch a value }

name: NmPreSendIndCommitCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax dword Nm_PreSendIndCommit (LONG& idInOut, WORD& dlcInOut, BYTE data[], DWORD& flagsInOut)
description: Description Directly before sending a NM message on the bus, this function is called to allow the CAPL program to change the actual message sent. Any message byte can be altered by the function. If this function does not return 1 , then the message will not be sent. (Only for advanced users!).
parameters: Parameters id CAN identifier of message to be sent (alterable). dlc The length in bytes of the message to be sent (alterable). data The message data to be sent (alterable). flagsInOut Not implemented yet. Setting this param has no effect.
returnValues: Return Values 1 message will be sent Otherwise message will not be sent

name: NmReinitSleepTimerCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_ReinitSleepTimer(long isActive)
description: Description This function reinitializes the timer until NM releases the network. Calling has no effect if there is no interaction layer.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmRemoteSleepIndCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_RemoteSleepInd(long flag)
description: Description If no other node on the network has sent a NM message for a configurable amount of time, this function is called with flag==1. If later another node's NM message is seen on the network, this function is called again with flag==0.
parameters: Parameters flag The indication type: 0 : Indicates that Remote sleep state is left 1 : Indicates that Remote sleep state is entered
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Indicate whether other nodes allow the network to go to sleep. void Nm_RemoteSleepInd( long flag) { if( flag) Write( "Nm_RemoteSleepInd: Other nodes ready to sleep"); else Write( "Nm_RemoteSleepInd: Another node requests network"); }

name: NmRepeatMessageRequestCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_RepeatMessageRequest( )
description: Description Make the NM leave Normal Operation or Ready Sleep state and enter Repeat Message state, and set the repeat message bit.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmRequestBusSynchCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_RequestBusSynch( )
description: Description Send a NM message immediately to resynchronize all NM timeouts in the nodes on the network. The NM cycle timer is not restarted, i.e. the message is sent independently of the NM message cycle.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmSendImmediateNmPDUCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_SendImmediateNmPDU(dword flags, BYTE data[], dword bufferSize)
description: Description Send immediately a NM PDU with the provided payload.
parameters: Parameters flags Currently unused. data Data bytes including the protocol bytes of NM PDU. bufferSize Size of data buffer in bytes (value must be identical to regular NM PDU size).
returnValues: Return Values -1 invalid 0 not executed 1 OK 2 not available
example: Example on key 'x' { long result; BYTE pduData[8]; //modify bytes as desired, e.g. pduData[Nm_GetSniPosition()] = Nm_GetLocalNodeIdentifier(); pduData[Nm_GetCbvPosition()] = 0x40; result = Nm_SendImmediateNmPDU(0, pduData, elcount(pduData)); if (result == 1) { write("Sending immediate NM PDU successfully executed"); } }

name: NmSetApplicationActivityCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_SetApplicationActivity(long isActive)
description: Description This function sets the NM's application activity. If the flag is set (isActive = 1) and the network is sleeping, nothing will happen. But if the network wakes up later, it will stay active until the flag is reset again. If the flag is set and the network is active, it will stay active until the flag is reset again.
parameters: Parameters isActive 0 : no activity 1 : activity
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (no change of isActive ) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmSetAutoStartParamCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_SetAutoStartParam(dword autoStartParam)
description: Description Define the behavior of NM at measurement start. If used at all, the function must be called within the on preStart event handler of a CAPL Program.
parameters: Parameters autoStartParam Determines the start up behavior: 0 : No automatic network request at measurement start by NM (network requests via CAPL or from the interaction layer might still lead to a transition into repeat message at measurement start) 1 : Automatic network request at measurement start 2 : Automatic network request if interaction layer is present, no automatic network request in case interaction layer is absent
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmSetCarWakeUpBitCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_SetCarWakeUpBit(long value)
description: Description Modify the car wake-up bit in the user data. Is only available when the car wake-up bit is configured (else, 0 will be returned).
parameters: Parameters value The desired boolean value (0 = false, >0 = true).
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmSetControlBitVectorCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_SetControlBitVector(dword controlBits)
description: Description This function allows setting the Control Bit Vector byte sent by this node to any value.
parameters: Parameters controlBits The desired CBV, which is interpreted bit-wise.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmSetILSleepTimeCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_SetILSleepTime(dword timeMS)
description: Description This function changes the value for next reinitialization of the sleep timer. An on-going timer is not influenced.
parameters: Parameters timeMS new value of the timer in milliseconds
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (no IL) -1 error

name: NmSetPnRequestBitsCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_SetPnRequestBits(BYTE requestVector[])
description: Description Set the partial network request bits (the PN info range ) within the user data. All relevant parameters must be available ( INI Files Partial Network ) or the function call will fail.
parameters: Parameters requestVector The desired PN request bits, which are interpreted bit-wise. Must have the same length as the configured PN info range.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmSetUserDataCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_SetUserData(BYTE userData[], dword count)
description: Description Set the user data this node sends in its NM message to the byte sequence given, where only the bytes after node identifier or Control Bit Vector are changed.
parameters: Parameters userData User data to be set. bufferSize Length of the user data buffer (cannot exceed the configured user data length).
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmSetVerbosityCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax dword Nm_SetVerbosity(dword selector)
description: Description Set the modeling library 's verbosity. The higher the value, the more information is printed in the Write Window.
parameters: Parameters level The desired verbosity level: 0 : Report only critical errors 1 : Report errors resulting from misconfiguration etc. 2 : Warn if an unexpected protocol state is reached and report missing mandatory callbacks 3 : Inform of missing optional callbacks and minor issues >3 : Report low-level events like timer setup and expiration
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmStateChangeNotificationCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_StateChangeNotification(long previousState, long currentState)
description: Description Every time the NM state machine changes from one state to another, this function will be called if it is defined.
parameters: Parameters previousState The NM state which is left. currentState The NM state which is entered. (Please refer to the NM State functions for the table of values.)
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Indicate the old and new state in the Write Window. void Nm_StateChangeNotification( long previousState, long currentState) { Write( "Nm_StateChangeNotification: From %d to %d" , previousState, currentState); }

name: NmSynchronizedPncShutdownPduRxIndCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax void Nm_SynchronizedPncShutdownPduRxInd(dword busContext, dword shortId, dword longId, char name[], dword flags, BYTE data[])
description: Description A call of this function indicates the reception of a NM PDU with a valid synchronized PNC shutdown in the Control Bit Vector (CBV). A synchronized PNC shutdown is only valid if the PNI bit as well as the bit for synchronized shutdown are set simultaneously.
parameters: Parameters busContext Bus context of the modeling library calling this function (only relevant for gateway nodes). shortId If AUTOSAR PDUs are used: short header ID of the AUTOSAR PDU. Otherwise: CAN ID of received frame. longId If AUTOSAR PDUs are used: long header ID of the AUTOSAR PDU. Otherwise: CAN ID of received frame. name If AUTOSAR PDUs are used: name of the AUTOSAR PDU. Otherwise: empty string. flags Currently unused. data Data bytes including the protocol bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmTransmitCAN330
syntax: Function Syntax long Nm_Transmit( )
description: Description Send a NM message immediately and restart the NM cycle timer.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values The specification defines that Nm_Transmit shall only be available under certain conditions. This is indicated by the return value: 1 OK 0 not executed (sending not possible because of the node state) 2 feature is not activated ( non-standard behaviour ) -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: DisableCommunicationFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_DisableCommunication(dword channelMask, dword pduType)
description: Description This function disables the sending of NM messages. The parameters allow to specify the affected messages.
parameters: Parameters channelMask 1 : FR A 2 : FR B 3 : FR AB otherwise: no impact pduType 0 : all kind of PDUs 1 : Vote PDU 2 : Data PDU 3 : Sync PDU otherwise: no impact If Vote and Data are in the same PDU, the parameter pduType = 2 must be used in order to manipulate the sending behavior.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: EnableCommunicationFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_EnableCommunication(dword channelMask, dword pduType)
description: Description This function enables the sending of NM messages. The parameters allow to specify the affected messages.
parameters: Parameters channelMask 1 : FR A 2 : FR B 3 : FR AB otherwise: no impact pduType 0 : all kind of PDUs 1 : Vote PDU 2 : Data PDU 3 : Sync PDU otherwise: no impact If Vote and Data are in the same PDU, the parameter pduType = 2 must be used in order to manipulate the sending behavior.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: FrNmSynchronizeModeIndFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void FrNm_SynchronizeModeInd( )
description: Description This function indicate that the NM has entered the respective mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example The new mode is indicated in the Write Window. void FrNm_SynchronizeModeInd() { Write( "Nm_SynchronizeModeInd: Synchronize Mode"); }

name: NmBusSleepModeIndFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_BusSleepModeInd( )
description: Description This function indicates that the NM has entered the respective mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example The new mode is indicated in the Write Window. void Fr Nm_BusSleepModeInd() { Write( "Nm_BusSleepModeInd: Sleep Mode"); }

name: NmBusSleepModePreIndFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void FrNm_BusSleepModePreInd(long valid, long deltaCycles, long absCycle)
description: Description This indication is invoked a specific number of FlexRay cycles before the NM will go into state BusSleepMode. It is also called in case that an indication priorly given is not valid any more (e.g. due to a node which keeps the bus awake now).
parameters: Parameters valid 0 : A priorly given indication is not valid any more 1 : Indication that NM state BusSleepMode will be entered in a specific number of FlexRay cycles deltaCycles Number of FlexRay cycles until the NM state BusSleepMode will be entered (equal to parameter ReadyPreSleepCnt in INI file) absCycle FlexRay cycle at which the NM state BusSleepMode will be entered
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmCheckRemoteSleepIndFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_CheckRemoteSleepInd( )
description: Description Check if remote sleep (all nodes except this one allow transition to Bus-Sleep) has been detected, i.e. this node is the last one keeping the bus awake.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 remote sleep is indicated 0 remote sleep is not indicated

name: NmConfigureILNotificationsFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_ConfigureILNotifications(dword direction, dword mode)
description: Description This function influences the coupling between NM and IL. The coupling normally takes care of forwarding certain events automatically. Implementing CAPL callback functions of the IL and NM may break open this coupling.
parameters: Parameters direction specifies which events are affected 0 : Events from NM to IL (e.g. network mode indication) 1 : Events from IL to NM (e.g. network request due to clamp 15) mode specifies the according effect, the value is interpreted bitwise Bit 0 0 : Open – events are not propagated automatically 1 : Fixed – events are propagated automatically even in case of according CAPL callback functions (does not work for direction = 1)
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (wrong parameter values or illegal combination)

name: NmControlBitVectorIndFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_ControlBitVectorInd(dword sourceId, dword controlBits)
description: Description This function is called if a NM dataPDu with a non-zero Control-Bit-Vector is received. In addition to the vector itself, the identifier of the sender node is given.
parameters: Parameters sourceId The NM source identifier in the NM PDU or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. controlBits The Control Bit Vector received.
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Print the identifier of the sending node in the Write Window. void Fr Nm_ControlBitVectorInd( dword sourceId, dword controlBits) { Write( " Fr Nm_ControlBitVectorInd: from node %d, bits %02x" , sourceId, controlBits); }

name: NmCtrlSimulationOffFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_CtrlSimulationOff( )
description: Description This function will turn off the complete NM simulation by the modeling library . While the NM simulation is off, any API call (except Fr Nm_CtrlSimulationOn() ) will not have any effect and the modeling library will not sent any NM PDU !
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed

name: NmCtrlSimulationOnFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_CtrlSimulationOn( )
description: Description This function will turn on the complete NM simulation by the modeling library . While the NM simulation is off, any API call (except Fr Nm_CtrlSimulationOn() ) will not have any effect and the modeling library will not sent any NM PDU !
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed

name: NmDisablePassiveModeFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_DisablePassiveMode( )
description: Description Stop using the passive mode: During Bus-Sleep, a node may be put into passive mode, or back to active mode. In passive mode, the node will then never send NM messages, but receive the NM messages from other nodes and compute the network's state accordingly.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed

name: NmEnablePassiveModeFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_EnablePassiveMode( )
description: Description Start using the passive mode: During Bus-Sleep, a node may be put into passive mode, or back to active mode. In passive mode, the node will then never send NM messages, but receive the NM messages from other nodes and compute the network's state accordingly.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 1 : OK 0 0 : not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmFIIgnoreRepeatMessageBitFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr NmFI_IgnoreRepeatMessageBit(long value)
description: Description Defines the reaction upon received repeat message bit.
parameters: Parameters 0 normal behavior 1 repeat message bit will be ignored
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmGetCbvPositionFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetCbvPosition( )
description: Description Returns the byte position (zero-based numbering) of the Control Bit Vector (CBV) within the NM PDU.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 NM PDU does not contain CBV. ≥ 0 Byte position of CBV within NM PDU.

name: NmGetControlBitVectorFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_GetControlBitVector(dword &controlBits)
description: Description This function allows setting the Control Bit Vector byte sent by this node to any value.
parameters: Parameters controlBits Reference parameter to read the CBV.
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (Simulation is not enabled, or no CBV is used)
example: Example dword out; long ret; ret = Fr Nm_GetControlBitVector(out); if (ret == 1) { write("CBV value: %u", out); }

name: NmGetDLLInfoFR301
syntax: Function Syntax dword Fr Nm_GetDLLInfo(dword selector)
description: Description Return additional information about the modeling library .
parameters: Parameters selector Selects different return values (see below).
returnValues: Return Values selector = 0 The modeling library 's file version as BCD, e.g. 0x010203 for V1.2.3 selector = 1 The modeling library 's major version selector = 2 The modeling library 's minor version selector = 3 The modeling library 's build number

name: NmGetLocalNodeIdentifierFR301
syntax: Function Syntax dword Fr Nm_GetLocalNodeIdentifier( )
description: Description Return the NM identifier of this node.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values Node identifier, range 0x00 – 0xFF

name: NmGetNodeIdentifierFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetNodeIdentifier( )
description: Description Get the NM identifier of the node that sent the last received NM message.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0x00 .. 0xFF node identifier -1 error (e.g. no message has been received so far, function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetNodeNameFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_GetNodeName(char bufferOut[], dword bufferSize)
description: Description The FIBEX database name of this node is copied into the provided buffer.
parameters: Parameters bufferOut Output buffer for the node name. bufferSize Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmGetNodeStateFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetNodeState(dword nodeId)
description: Description Check if the node with the given NM identifier has sent a NM message the last time the network was in Repeat Message state. Precondition for retrieval of meaningful information in a node is a preceding call of the function Fr Nm_RepeatMessageRequest and the completion of the subsequent repeat message phase.
parameters: Parameters nodeId Target node ID, range 0x00 – 0xFF
returnValues: Return Values 2 information is not (yet) available (e.g. node detection not started or ongoing) 1 target node has been detected 0 target node has not been detected -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetPduDataFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetPduData(BYTE pduOut[], dword bufferLen)
description: Description Copy the last received NM PDU completely into the buffer.
parameters: Parameters dataOut Output buffer for the user data. bufferLen Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values >=0 number of bytes copied -1 error (e.g. no message has been received so far, function is called in 'on preStart' or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetSniPositionFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetSniPosition ( )
description: Description Returns the byte position PDU (zero-based numbering) of the Source Node Identifier (SNI) within the NM PDU.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values -1 NM PDU does not contain SNI. ≥ 0 Byte position of the SNI within NM PDU.

name: NmGetStateFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetState( )
description: Description The returned value indicates the NM state the node is currently in.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 0 NM_STATE_UNINIT 1 NM_STATE_BUS_SLEEP 2 NM_STATE_PREPARE_BUS_SLEEP (never reached on FlexRay!) 3 NM_STATE_READY_SLEEP 4 NM_STATE_NORMAL_OPERATION 5 NM_STATE_REPEAT_MESSAGE 6 NM_STATE_SYNCHRONIZE -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmGetUserDataFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_GetUserData(BYTE dataOut[], dword bufferLen)
description: Description Copy the user data of the last received NM PDU into the output buffer.
parameters: Parameters dataOut Output buffer for the user data. bufferLen Size of the output buffer.
returnValues: Return Values >=0 number of bytes copied -1 error (e.g. no message has been received so far, function is called in 'on preStart' or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmInhibitActiveWakeUpBitFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_InhibitActiveWakeUpBit (long value)
description: Description By default, the active wake-up bit in the Control Bit Vector of the NM PDU is set. This function allows to prevent this. Function can be used in on preStart.
parameters: Parameters value 0 : Active wake-up bit in CBV is set (default). 1 : Active wake-up bit in CBV is not set.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmNetworkModeIndFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_NetworkModeInd( )
description: Description This function indicate that the NM has entered the respective mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example The new mode is indicated in the Write Window. void Fr Nm_NetworkModeInd() { Write( "Nm_NetworkModeInd: Network Mode"); }

name: NmNetworkReleaseFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_NetworkRelease( )
description: Description Indicate that the NM node doesn't need the network, and allows it to enter Bus-Sleep mode.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmNetworkRequestFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_NetworkRequest( )
description: Description Indicate that the NM node needs the network.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmNetworkStartIndFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_NetworkStartInd( )
description: Description A NM PDU has been received while the node is in Bus-Sleep mode. Typically Fr Nm_PassiveStartUp( ) will be called to let the node enter Repeat-Message state.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Call start-up function to change to Repeat Message state. void Fr Nm_NetworkStartInd() { Write( " Fr Nm_NetworkStartInd: Network started"); Fr Nm_PassiveStartUp(); }

name: NmPassiveStartUpFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_PassiveStartUp( )
description: Description Make the NM leave the Bus-Sleep mode and enter the Repeat Message state, without actually requesting the bus.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmPduRxInd2FR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_PduRxInd2(dword busContext, dword shortId, dword longId, char name[], dword sourceNodeIdentifier, long repeatMessageBit, BYTE data[])
description: Description A call to this function indicates the reception of a NM message from another node. The arguments are taken from the NM message directly.
parameters: Parameters busContext Bus context of the node layer calling this function. shortId Short header ID of the AUTOSAR PDU. longId Long header ID of the AUTOSAR PDU. name Name of the AUTOSAR PDU. sourceNodeIdentifier The NM source identifier in the NM message or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. bRepeatMessageBit 1 : f the Repeat-Message bit is set 0 : otherwise data The message's data bytes including the protocol bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmPduRxIndFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_PduRxInd( dword slotId , dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[])
description: Description A call to this function indicates the reception of a NM data PDU from another node. The arguments are taken from the NM message directly.
parameters: Parameters slotId The slot ID of the message. identifier The NM source identifier in the NM PDU or 0, if the source identifier is not transferred. bRepeatMessageBit 1 if the Repeat-Message bit is set, 0 otherwise. data The message's data bytes including the protocol bytes.
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Report the NM message received in the Write Window. void Fr Nm_PduRxInd( dword slotId , dword identifier, long bRepeatMessageBit, BYTE data[]) { Write( " Fr Nm_PduRxInd: NM id=%d, RM=%d, %d byte" identifier, bRepeatMessageBit, elcount(data) ); }

name: NmPduTxIndFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_PduTxInd(dword channelMask, dword slotId, dword pduType, BYTE data[], long identifier, long repeatMessageBit, long vote)
description: Description This function is called after a NM PDU was sent on the bus.
parameters: Parameters channelMask 1 : FR A 2 : FR B slotID Slot in which the PDU was sent. pduType 1 : Vote PDU 2 : Data PDU 3 : Vote + Data PDU (for scheduling variant 1 and 2) data Content of the NM PDU (empty in case of pduType = 1). identifier NM source identifier in the NM PDU (-1 in case of pduType = 1). repeatMessageBit Repeat message bit in the NM PDU (-1 in case of pduType = 1). vote 0 : NM PDU is a vote PDU and does not vote to keep the bus awake 1 : NM PDU is a vote PDU and votes to keep the bus awake -1 : NM PDU is not a vote PDU (pduType = 2)
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmPreSendIndExtFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_PreSendIndExt(WORD dlcInOut[], BYTE data[], dword channelMask, dword pduType)
description: Description Directly before sending a NM message on the bus, this function is called to allow the CAPL program to change the actual message sent. Any message byte can be altered by the function to change the actual message sent or even suppress it.
parameters: Parameters dlcInOut (alterable) The DLC of the message to be sent. If the DLC is set to a value greater than 254 , the message will not be sent at all. data (alterable) The message data to be sent. channelMask (not alterable) 1 : FR A 2 : FR B pduType (not alterable) 1 : Vote PDU 2 : Data PDU 3 : Sync PDU
returnValues: Return Values > 0 message will be sent otherwise message will not be sent

name: NmPreSendIndFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_PreSendInd(WORD dlcInOut[], BYTE data[])
description: Description Directly before sending a NM message on the bus, this function is called to allow the CAPL program to change the actual message sent. Any message byte can be altered by the function to change the actual message sent or even suppress it .
parameters: Parameters dlcInOut The DLC of the message to be sent (alterable). If the DLC is set to a value greater than 254, the message will not be sent at all. data The message data to be sent (alterable).
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Every NM message sent by this node is patched to be 7 bytes long, and byte 6 is set to 0xFF. void Fr Nm_PreSendInd( WORD dlcInOut[], BYTE data[]) { dlcInOut[0] = 7; // change the DLC of the message sent data[6] = 0xFF; // patch a value }

name: NmReinitSleepTimerFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_ReinitSleepTimer(long isActive)
description: Description This function reinitializes the timer until NM releases the network. Calling has no effect if there is no interaction layer.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmRemoteSleepIndFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_RemoteSleepInd(long flag)
description: Description If no other node on the network has sent a NM PDU for a configurable amount of time, this function is called with flag==1. If later another node's NM PDU is seen on the network, this function is called again with flag==0.
parameters: Parameters flag The indication type: 0 : Indicates that Remote sleep state is left 1 : Indicates that Remote sleep state is entered
returnValues: Return Values —
example: Example Indicate whether other nodes allow the network to go to sleep. void Fr Nm_RemoteSleepInd( long flag) { if( flag) Write( " Fr Nm_RemoteSleepInd: Other nodes ready to sleep"); else Write( " Fr Nm_RemoteSleepInd: Another node requests network"); }

name: NmRepeatMessageRequestFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_RepeatMessageRequest( )
description: Description Make the NM leave Normal Operation or Ready Sleep state and enter Repeat Message state, and set the repeat message bit.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmRequestBusSynchFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_RequestBusSynch( )
description: Description Send a NM message immediately to resynchronize all NM timeouts in the nodes on the network. The NM cycle timer is not restarted, i.e. the message is sent independently of the NM message cycle.
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed -1 error (e.g. function is called in on preStart or protocol engine is inactive)

name: NmSendImmediateNmPDUFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_SendImmediateNmPDU(dword flags, BYTE data[], dword bufferSize)
description: Description Send a NM Data PDU with the provided payload on next opportunity of FlexRay schedule for this PDU.
parameters: Parameters flags Currently unused. data Data bytes including the protocol bytes of NM PDU. bufferSize Size of data buffer in bytes (value must be identical to regular NM PDU size).
returnValues: Return Values -1 invalid 0 not executed 1 OK 2 not available
example: Example on key 'x' { long result; BYTE pduData[8]; //modify bytes as desired, e.g. pduData[ Fr Nm_GetSniPosition()] = Fr Nm_GetLocalNodeIdentifier(); pduData[ Fr Nm_GetCbvPosition()] = 0x40; result = Fr Nm_SendImmediateNmPDU(0, pduData, elcount(pduData)); if (result == 1) { write("Sending immediate Fr NM PDU successfully executed"); } }

name: NmSetApplicationActivityFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_SetApplicationActivity(long isActive)
description: Description This function sets the NM's application activity. If the flag is set (isActive = 1) and the network is sleeping, nothing will happen. But if the network wakes up later, it will stay active until the flag is reset again. If the flag is set and the network is active, it will stay active until the flag is reset again.
parameters: Parameters isActive 0 : no activity 1 : activity
returnValues: Return Values 1 OK 0 not executed (no change of isActive )

name: NmSetAutoStartParamFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_SetAutoStartParam(dword autoStartParam)
description: Description Define the behavior of NM at measurement start. If used at all, the function must be called within the on preStart event handler of a CAPL Program.
parameters: Parameters autoStartParam Determines the start up behavior: 0 : No automatic network request at measurement start by NM (network requests via CAPL or from the interaction layer might still lead to a transition into repeat message at measurement start) 1 : Automatic network request at measurement start 2 : Automatic network request if interaction layer is present, no automatic network request in case interaction layer is absent
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmSetControlBitVectorFR301
syntax: Function Syntax void Fr Nm_SetControlBitVector(dword controlBits)
description: Description This function allows setting the Control Bit Vector byte sent by this node to any value.
parameters: Parameters controlBits The desired CBV, which is interpreted bit-wise.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: NmSetILSleepTimeFR301
syntax: Function Syntax long Fr Nm_SetILSleepTime(dword timeMS)
