In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_CANNOTADDDYNAMICPROVIDER The service does not allow dynamic provider creation.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclAnnounceProvider
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclAnnounceProvider(cclProviderID providerID);
description: Description Triggers a service provider to send service announcements.
parameters: Parameters providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS Service announcement is in progress (the provider is not yet necessarily connected). CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclAnnounceProviderToConsumer
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclAnnounceProviderToConsumer(cclServiceID serviceID, cclProviderID providerID, cclConsumerID consumerID);
description: Description Sends an acknowledgment for a specific service request.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of a service communication object. providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions). consumerID The ID of a service consumer (returned by cclAddConsumerByAddress , cclAddConsumerByName and cclConsumerGetID functions).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The service was successfully announced to the consumer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMERID The consumerID parameter does not identify a known service consumer object. CCL_INVALIDBINDING Either the provider or the consumer is not correctly bound.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallAsync
syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclCallHandler)(cclTime time, cclCallContextID ccID); int32_t cclCallAsync(cclCallContextID ccID, cclCallHandler resultHandler);
description: Description This function invokes a network function call and registers an event routine for the response.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context which holds the prepared input parameters of the call. Each call context can only be invoked once (while in state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLING) and if successful transitions to state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLED. resultHandler An event routine to be called back when the network function call has returned at the consumer side (it will then be in state CCL_CALL_STATE_RETURNED). Pass the literal nullptr if no event routine should be called back, e.g. if you have a global event routine registered for the network function port or if you are not interested in the result of the function call. The event routine receives the simulation time when the function call returned at consumer side and the call context ID for accessing results and output parameters.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The network function call was successfully invoked. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object. CCL_INVALIDSIDE The call context must identify a consumer side network function call port. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTSTATE The call context must be in state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLING, which is the result of function cclCreateCallContext .
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextGetFunctionID
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCallContextGetFunctionID(cclCallContextID ccID, cclFunctionID* functionID);
description: Description Returns the network function port ID associated with a call context.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context for which to obtain the network function port. functionID The ID of the network function port is written to this variable.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The network function port ID was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextGetFunctionName
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCallContextGetFunctionName(cclCallContextID ccID, char* buffer, int32_t bufferSize);
description: Description Returns the network function communication object name associated with a call context.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context for which to obtain the network function name. buffer The name of the network function is written to this buffer in UTF-8 encoding. bufferSize The size of the allocated buffer for the name (including the terminating 0).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The network function name was successfully written to the buffer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object. CCL_BUFFERTOSMALL The network function's name doesn't fit the given buffer size.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextGetFunctionPath
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCallContextGetFunctionPath(cclCallContextID ccID, char* buffer, int32_t bufferSize);
description: Description Returns the network function port path associated with a call context.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context for which to obtain the network function port path. buffer The port path of the network function is written to this buffer in UTF-8 encoding. bufferSize The size of the allocated buffer for the name (including the terminating 0).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The network function port path was successfully written to the buffer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object. CCL_BUFFERTOSMALL The network function port path doesn't fit the given buffer size.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextIsSynchronousCall
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCallContextIsSynchronousCall(cclCallContextID ccID, bool* isSynchronous);
description: Description Returns whether a specific call context was used for a synchronous call. It is meant to be used during a call handler registered with cclFunctionSetHandler for a distributed object method.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context which is currently active. isSynchronous Out parameter which will be set to true if the call was made synchronously and false otherwise. In particular, it will be set to false if the call was not yet made.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was successful. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object.

name: cclCallContextMakePermanent
syntax: Function Syntax cclCallContextID cclCallContextMakePermanent(cclCallContextID ccID);
description: Description Creates a permanent clone of a call context, which needs explicit release via function cclCallContextRelease and can be used beyond the current program scope. This is typically only applied to call contexts passed into event routines, which are valid only for the execution of that routine. All not released permanent call contexts are automatically removed at measurement end but you should be aware that repeated permanent call context creation from an event routine may lead to accumulating memory consumption.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context to be cloned (typically a parameter to an event routine).
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the permanent call context. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextRelease
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCallContextRelease(cclCallContextID ccID);
description: Description Releases a permanent call context. The call context ID cannot be used anymore during the measurement. All not released permanent call contexts are automatically removed at measurement end but you should be aware that repeated permanent call context creation from an event routine may lead to accumulating memory consumption.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the permanent call context to be released (returned by cclCallContextMakePermanent ).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The call context was successfully released. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextReturn
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCallContextReturn(cclCallContextID ccID, cclTime timeOffset);
description: Description Sets the relative simulation time when a network function call shall return at provider side. This function must only be invoked from an event routine handling the provider side state transition to CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLED, i.e. when simulating the provider side function implementation. If no return time is set in such a handler, the function call will return immediately.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the network function call being simulated. timeOffset The time at which the function call should return at provider side, relative to the current simulation time. You can specify the constant CCL_TIME_OFFSET_NEVER to defer the response for an indeterminate time.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The return time was successfully set. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTSTATE The call context must be in state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLED. CCL_INVALIDSIDE The call context must identify a provider side network function port. CCL_INVALIDTIME The timeOffset parameter must greater than or equal to 0 or the constant CCL_TIME_OFFSET_NEVER.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextSetDefaultAnswer
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCallContextSetDefaultAnswer(cclCallContextID ccID);
description: Description Copies the simulation server configured default result and output parameters to the given call context. This function must only be invoked from an event routine handling the provider side state transition to CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLED, i.e. when simulating the provider side function implementation.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the network function call being simulated.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The result and out parameters were successfully set to defaults. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTSTATE The call context must be in state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLED. CCL_INVALIDSIDE The call context must identify a provider side network function port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallContextValueGetID
syntax: Function Syntax cclValueID cclCallContextValueGetID(cclCallContextID ccID, const char* member, cclValueRepresentation repr);
description: Description Returns a communication object value ID, which can be used for setting and reading call context parameter values and state. The values are only valid while the context itself is valid (especially call context parameters of event routines are valid only within that routine).
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context in which a parameter shall be accessed. member Path to a parameter, state or sub-member of such. Any member path into a call context must start with one of the predefined constants CCL_MEMBER_IN_PARAM concatenated with an input parameter name and an optional sub-member path CCL_MEMBER_OUT_PARAM concatenated with an output parameter name and an optional sub-member path CCL_MEMBER_RESULT concatenated with an optional sub-member path for accessing the network function's return value CCL_MEMBER_CALL_TIME for accessing the simulation time when the network function was called (integer value) CCL_MEMBER_RETURN_TIME for accessing the simulation time when the network function returned a result (integer value) CCL_MEMBER_CALL_STATE for accessing the current state of the network function call (enumerated value from cclCallState) CCL_MEMBER_REQUEST_ID for accessing the internal network function call's request identifier (integer value) CCL_MEMBER_SIDE for accessing the call context's processing side (enumerated value from cclSide) CCL_MEMBER_CONSUMER_PORT for accessing the network function's consumer side port path (string value) CCL_MEMBER_PROVIDER_PORT
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the value. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_PARAMETERINVALID The repr parameter must be one of the enumerated values in cclValueRepresentation. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object. CCL_INVALIDMEMBERPATH The member parameter does not identify a valid call context value member.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCallSync
syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclCallHandler)(cclTime time, cclCallContextID ccID); int32_t cclCallSync(cclCallContextID ccID, cclCallHandler resultHandler);
description: Description This function synchronously invokes a distributed object method call and registers an event routine for the response. The function can only be called for internal distributed object methods and specific methods provided by bindings. In contrast to cclCallAsync , it invokes the method synchronously. The resultHandler will therefore be called before the call to cclCallAsync returns.
parameters: Parameters ccID ID of the call context which holds the prepared input parameters of the call. Each call context can only be invoked once (while in state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLING) and if successful transitions to state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLED. resultHandler An event routine to be called back when the method call has returned (it will then be in state CCL_CALL_STATE_RETURNED). Pass the literal nullptr if no event routine should be called back, e.g. if you are not interested in the result of the function call. The event routine receives the simulation time when the function call returned and the call context ID for accessing results and output parameters.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The network function call was successfully invoked. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTID The ccID parameter does not identify a known call context object. CCL_INVALIDSIDE The call context must identify a consumer side network function call port. CCL_INVALIDCALLCONTEXTSTATE The call context must be in state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLING, which is the result of function cclCreateCallContext . CCL_NO_SYNCHRONOUS_CALL The function may not be called synchronously, e.g. because it is a consumed DO distributed object method or a CO communication object method. Use cclCallAsync instead.

name: cclCanGetChannelNumber
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCanGetChannelNumber(const char* networkName, int32_t* channel);
description: Description Retrieves the CAN channel number for the given network name.
parameters: Parameters networkName The name of the network, for which a CAN channel should be retrieved. Channel Pointer to a variable, where the function should store the retrieved CAN channel.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The channel was successfully retrieved. CCL_NETWORKNOTDEFINED The CANoe configuration does not contain a CAN network with the given name. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED Functionality is not implemented, version 11.0 SP3 or newer is required. CCL_INVALIDNAME The networkName parameter is a NULL pointer or an empty string.
example: Example int32_t gChannelChassis = 0; void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; rc = cclCanGetChannelNumber("Chassies", &gChannelChassis); }

name: cclCanIsExtendedIdentifier
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCanIsExtendedIdentifier(uint32_t identifier);
description: Description Checks parameter for extended CAN identifier (29 bit). Inside the program, a CAN identifier is saved as 32 bit unsigned integer, for which the most significant bit determines whether it is a 29 bit extended identifier or an 11 bit standard identifier.
parameters: Parameters identifier CAN identifier
returnValues: Return Values This function returns 1 if the parameter describes an extended CAN identifier (11 bit). Otherwise, it returns 0 .

name: cclCanIsStandardIdentifier
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCanIsStandardIdentifier(uint32_t identifier);
description: Description Checks parameter for standard CAN identifier (11 Bit). Inside the program, a CAN identifier is saved as 32-bit unsigned integer, for which the most significant bit determines whether it is a 29-bit extended identifier or an 11-bit standard identifier.
parameters: Parameters identifier CAN identifier
returnValues: Return Values This function returns 1 if the parameter describes an standard CAN identifier (11-bit). Otherwise, it returns 0 .

name: cclCanMakeExtendedIdentifier
syntax: Function Syntax uint32_t cclCanMakeExtendedIdentifier(uint32_t identifier);
description: Description This function creates an extended identifier (29-bit) from the specified value. Inside the program, a CAN identifier is saved as 32-bit unsigned integer, for which the most significant bit determines whether it is a 29-bit extended identifier or an 11-bit standard identifier.
parameters: Parameters identifier Value of the extended identifier.
returnValues: Return Values CAN identifier as required for the function cclCanSetMessageHandler .

name: cclCanMakeStandardIdentifier
syntax: Function Syntax uint32_t cclCanMakeStandardIdentifier(uint32_t identifier);
description: Description This function creates an standard identifier (11-bit) from the specified value. Inside the program, a CAN identifier is saved as 32-bit unsigned integer, for which the most significant bit determines whether it is a 29-bit extended identifier or an 11-bit standard identifier.
parameters: Parameters identifier Value of the standard identifier.
returnValues: Return Values CAN identifier as required for the function cclCanSetMessageHandler .

name: cclCanOutputMessage
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclCanOutputMessage(int32_t channel, uint32_t identifier, uint32_t flags, uint8_t dataLength, const uint8_t data[]);
description: Description This function outputs a message over the CAN bus. Calling this function is not blocking, i.e. the function only writes the message in the send buffer of the CAN driver and then returns immediately. The actual sending of the message occurs asynchronously with the program flow of the C library.
parameters: Parameters channel The CAN channel on which the message is to be output. identifier The CAN identifier of the message. The most-significant bit of this parameter determines whether it is a 29-bit extended identifier or an 11-bit standard identifier. Use the functions cclCanMakeExtendedIdentifier and cclCanMakeStandardIdentifier to create this parameter. flags Using the parameter flag, some additional properties of the message to be sent can be set: CCL_CANFLAGS_RTR Protocol Bit RTR (Remote Transmission Request, for classic CAN only). If this bit is set (1), the message is sent as a remote frame CCL_CANFLAGS_WAKEUP The message is sent in High Voltage Mode . CCL_CANFLAGS_FDF Protocol Bit FDF (FD Frame Indicator). If the bit is set, the CAN frame uses the CAN FD format, otherwise the classic CAN format is used. CCL_CANFLAGS_BRS Protocol Bit BRS (Bitrate Switch, for CAN FD only). If this flag is not set (0), then there is no change of the bitrate for the data phase. If this flag is set (1), there is a change to a higher bitrate for the data phase. dataLength Data length in bytes of the message to be sent. data The data field of the message to be sent.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified CAN channel is invalid.

name: cclCanSetMessageHandler
syntax: Function Syntax struct cclCanMessage { int64_t time; int32_t channel; uint32_t id; uint32_t flags; uint8_t dir; uint8_t dataLength; uint8_t data[64]; }; int32_t cclCanSetMessageHandler(int32_t channel, uint32_t identifier, void (*function) (struct cclCanMessage* message) );
description: Struct cclCanMessage The struct that is transferred to the event routine, contains the following fields: time Time stamp (end-of-frame) of the event in nanoseconds. channel The CAN channel on which the message was received or sent. id CAN identifier of the message. The most significant bit of this parameter determines whether it is a 29-bit extended identifier or an 11-bit standard identifier. Using the functions cclCanIsExtendedIdentifier and cclCanIsStandardIdentifier , you can determine whether or not it is a standard identifier or an extended identifier. The function cclCanValueOfIdentifier extracts the value of the identifier regardless of its type. flags Using the flags, some additional properties of the CAN message can be determined. CCL_CANFLAGS_RTR Protocol Bit RTR (Remote Transmission Request, for classic CAN only). If this bit is set (1), the frame is a remote transmission request. CCL_CANFLAGS_WAKEUP The message was sent in High Voltage Mode . CCL_CANFLAGS_TE The NERR flag of the CAN transceiver was active upon reception of the message (system is in single-wire mode). CCL_CANFLAGS_FDF Protocol Bit FDF (FD Frame Indicator). If the bit is set, the CAN frame uses the CAN F
parameters: Parameters channel The CAN channel for which the event routine is to be active. identifier The CAN identifier of the message for which the event routine is to be registered. The most significant bit of this parameter determines whether it is a 29-bit extended identifier or an 11-bit standard identifier. Use the functions cclCanMakeExtendedIdentifier and cclCanMakeStandardIdentifier to create this parameter. The special value CCL_CAN_ALLMESSAGES registers the event routine for all messages of the specified CAN channel. function The event routine to be registered. The event routine is called as soon as an appropriate CAN message has been received or sent.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event routine was registered successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified CAN channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer.

name: cclCanValueOfIdentifier
syntax: Function Syntax uint32_t cclCanValueOfIdentifier(uint32_t identifier)
description: Description Returns the value of a CAN identifier. Inside the program, a CAN identifier is saved as 32-bit unsigned integer, for which the most significant bit determines whether it is a 29-bit extended identifier or an 11-bit standard identifier.
parameters: Parameters identifier CAN identifier
returnValues: Return Values Value of the CAN identifiers.

name: cclConnectAsyncConsumer
syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclConnectionEstablishedHandler)(); typedef void(*cclConnectionFailedHandler)(const char* error); int32_t cclConnectAsyncConsumer(cclConsumedServiceID consumedServiceID, cclConnectionEstablishedHandler success, cclConnectionFailedHandler failure);
description: Description
parameters: Parameters consumedServiceID The ID of a consumer side service port (returned by cclConsumedServiceGetID function). success Event routine to be called back when the connection was successfully established. failure Event routine to be called back when the connection failed. This event routine receives an error message string indicating the cause of the connection failure.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The consumer side service port is trying to establish a service connection. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDSERVICEID The consumedServiceID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service port object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConnectAsyncProvider
syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclConnectionEstablishedHandler)(); typedef void(*cclConnectionFailedHandler)(const char* error); int32_t cclConnectAsyncProvider(cclProvidedServiceID providedServiceID, cclConnectionEstablishedHandler success, cclConnectionFailedHandler failure);
description: Description Tries to establish a service connection from a provider side service port.
parameters: Parameters providedServiceID The ID of a provider side service port (returned by cclProvidedServiceGetID function). success Event routine to be called back when the connection was successfully established. failure Event routine to be called back when the connection failed. This event routine receives an error message string indicating the cause of the connection failure.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The provider side service port is trying to establish a service connection. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDSERVICEID The providedServiceID parameter does not identify a known provider side service port object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConsumedEventGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclConsumedEventID; cclConsumedEventID cclConsumedEventGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a consumer side service event port ID, which can be used in event registration functions. All event port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a consumer side service event port. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the event port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDEVENTPATH The path parameter does not identify a consumer side service event port known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConsumedEventSetSubscriptionStateIsolated
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclConsumedEventSetSubscriptionStateIsolated(cclConsumedEventID consumedEventID, bool subscribed);
description: Description Sets the low level subscription state of a consumer side service event port.
parameters: Parameters consumedEventID The ID of a consumer side service event port (returned by cclConsumedEventGetID function). subscribed If this parameter is set to true, the event port will transition to state CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_SUBSCRIBED, otherwise it will transition back to CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_AVAILABLE.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The subscription state was successfully changed. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDEVENTID The consumedEventID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service event port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConsumedFieldGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclConsumedFieldID; cclConsumedFieldID cclConsumedFieldGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a consumer side service field port ID, which can be used in field registration functions. All field port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a consumer side service field port. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the field port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDFIELDPATH The path parameter does not identify a consumer side service field port known to the system. CCL_FIELDNOTSUBSCRIBABLE The field does not have a notification and therefore cannot be used in field registration functions.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConsumedFieldSetSubscriptionStateIsolated
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclConsumedFieldSetSubscriptionStateIsolated(cclConsumedFieldID consumedFieldID, bool subscribed);
description: Description Sets the low level subscription state of a consumer side service field port.
parameters: Parameters consumedFieldID The ID of a consumer side service field port (returned by cclConsumedFieldGetID function). subscribed If this parameter is set to true, the field port will transition to state CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_SUBSCRIBED, otherwise it will transition back to CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_AVAILABLE.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The subscription state was successfully changed. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDFIELDID The consumedFieldID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service field port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConsumedPDUGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclPDUReceiverID; cclPDUReceiverID cclConsumedPDUGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a consumer side service PDU port ID, which can be used for accessing PDU signal values and in PDU registration functions. Consumer side service PDU port IDs can be used in place of receiver side non-service PDU port IDs but not vice-versa. All PDU port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a consumer side service PDU port. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the PDU port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDPDUPATH The path parameter does not identify a consumer side service PDU port known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConsumedPDUSetSubscriptionStateIsolated
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclConsumedPDUSetSubscriptionStateIsolated(cclPDUReceiverID consumedPDUID, bool subscribed);
description: Description Sets the low level subscription state of a consumer side service PDU port.
parameters: Parameters consumedPDUID The ID of a consumer side service PDU port (returned by cclConsumedPDUGetID function). subscribed If this parameter is set to true, the PDU port will transition to state CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_SUBSCRIBED, otherwise it will transition back to CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_AVAILABLE.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The subscription state was successfully changed. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDPDUID The consumedPDUID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service PDU port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConsumedServiceGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclConsumedServiceID; cclConsumedServiceID cclConsumedServiceGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a consumer side service port ID, which can be used in service discovery functions. All service port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a consumer side service port. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the consumer side service port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEPATH The path parameter does not identify a consumer side service port known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConsumedServiceValueGetID
syntax: Function Syntax cclValueID cclConsumedServiceValueGetID(cclConsumedServiceID consumedServiceID, const char* member, cclValueRepresentation repr);
description: Description Returns the communication object value identifier for a consumer side service port. The function is a convenience shortcut for accessing service port parameters when the service port is already available and its path is unknown.
parameters: Parameters consumedServiceID The ID of consumer side service port for which to access a value (e.g. connection state). member A member path for addressing a specific value of the port (only CCL_MEMBER_CONNECTION_STATE for consumer side service ports). repr The representation parameter can take the values CCL_IMPL, CCL_RAW or CCL_PHYS for access to the application implementation level, network transport or physical representation of the value.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the value. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDSERVICEID The consumedServiceID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service port object. CCL_INVALIDMEMBERPATH The member path does not identify a valid value of the service port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConsumerGetBindingID
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclConsumerGetBindingID(cclConsumerID consumerID, int32_t* bindingID);
description: Description Returns a binding specific identifier for a service consumer, which can be used to access ports of dynamically added consumers.
parameters: Parameters consumerID The ID of a service consumer (returned by cclAddConsumerByAddress , cclAddConsumerByName and cclConsumerGetID functions). bindingID The binding specific identifier is written to this variable.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The binding ID was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMERID The consumerID parameter does not identify a known service consumer object. CCL_CONSUMERNOTBOUND The consumer must be bound to an address.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclConsumerGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclConsumerID; cclConsumerID cclConsumerGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a consumer side service endpoint ID, which can be used in service discovery functions. All consumer IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a service consumer. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the service consumer. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEPATH The path parameter does not identify a service consumer known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclCreateCallContext
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclCallContextID; cclCallContextID cclCreateCallContext(cclFunctionID functionID);
description: Description Creates a call context, which is typically the preparation of a network function call. The context is initialized with the latest call input parameters and can subsequently be configured with input parameter values before invoking the network function call. The function can only be called on consumer side network function ports. The returned call context is initially in state CCL_CALL_STATE_CALLING.
parameters: Parameters functionID ID of the network function (returned by cclFunctionGetID).
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the call context. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONID The functionID parameter does not identify a known function object. CCL_INVALIDSIDE The functionID parameter must identify a consumer side network function port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclDisconnectConsumer
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclDisconnectConsumer(cclConsumedServiceID consumedServiceID);
description: Description Tears down a service connection from a consumer side service port.
parameters: Parameters consumedServiceID The ID of a consumer side service port (returned by cclConsumedServiceGetID functions).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The connection was successfully terminated. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDSERVICEID The consumedServiceID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service port object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclDisconnectProvider
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclDisconnectProvider(cclProvidedServiceID providedServiceID);
description: Description Tears down a service connection from a provider side service port.
parameters: Parameters providedServiceID The ID of a provider side service port (returned by cclProvidedServiceGetID function).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The connection was successfully terminated. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDSERVICEID The providedServiceID parameter does not identify a known provider side service port object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclDiscoverProviders
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclDiscoverProviders(cclConsumerID consumerID);
description: Description Triggers a service consumer to send service discovery requests.
parameters: Parameters consumerID The ID of a service consumer (returned by cclAddConsumerByAddress , cclAddConsumerByName and cclConsumerGetID functions).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS Service discovery request was successfully initiated (the consumer is not yet necessarily connected). CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMERID The consumerID parameter does not identify a known service consumer object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclEthernetGetChannelNumber
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclEthernetGetChannelNumber(const char* networkName, int32_t* channel);
description: Description Retrieves the Ethernet channel number for the given network name.
parameters: Parameters networkName The name of the network, for which an Ethernet channel should be retrieved. channel Pointer to a variable, where the function should store the retrieved Ethernet channel.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The channel was successfully retrieved. CCL_NETWORKNOTDEFINED The CANoe configuration does not contain an Ethernet network with the given name. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED Functionality is not implemented, version 15 SP2 or newer is required. CCL_INVALIDNAME The networkName parameter is a NULL pointer or an empty string.
example: Example int32_t gChannel = 0; void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; rc = cclEthernetGetChannelNumber("Ethernet1", &gChannel); }

name: cclEthernetOutputPacket
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclEthernetOutputPacket(int32_t channel, uint32_t packetSize, const uint8_t data[]);
description: Description This function outputs a packet over the Ethernet bus. Calling this function is not blocking, i.e., the function only writes the message in the send buffer of the Ethernet driver and then returns immediately. The actual sending of the message occurs asynchronously with the program flow of the C library.
parameters: Parameters channel The Ethernet channel on which the message is to be output. packetSize Data length in bytes of the packet to be sent. data The data of the packet to be sent.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The channel was successfully retrieved. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified Ethernet channel is invalid. CCL_NOTSUPPORTED The configuration uses a network with switched-based access.

name: cclEthernetSetPacketHandler
syntax: Function Syntax struct cclEthernetPacket { int64_t time; int32_t channel; uint8_t dir; uint8_t packetSize; uint8_t *packetData; }; int32_t cclEthernetSetPacketHandler(int32_t channel, void (*function) (struct cclEthernetPacket* packet) );
description: Description Registration of an event routine for the reception of Ethernet packets. Registration of the handler may only be carried out in the PreStartHandler. The registered event routines are unregistered automatically at the end of measurement.
parameters: Parameters channel The Ethernet channel for which the event routine is to be active. function The event routine to be registered. The event routine is called as soon as an Ethernet packet has been received or sent.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event routine was registered successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified Ethernet channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer. CCL_NOTSUPPORTED The configuration uses a network with switched-based access.

name: cclEventGetNrOfSubscribedConsumers
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclEventGetNrOfSubscribedConsumers(cclEventProviderID eventProviderID, int32_t* nrOfConsumers);
description: Description Returns the number of consumers currently subscribed at given service event provider.
parameters: Parameters eventProviderID The ID of a service event provider (returned by cclEventProviderGetID function). nrOfConsumers This variable returns the number of currently subscribed consumers.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The number of subscribed consumers was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDEVENTPROVIDERID The eventProviderID parameter does not identify a known service event provider.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclEventGetSubscribedConsumers
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclEventGetSubscribedConsumers(cclEventProviderID eventProviderID, cclConsumerID* consumerBuffer, int32_t* bufferSize);
description: Description Returns a list of the consumers currently subscribed at given service event provider.
parameters: Parameters eventProviderID The ID of a service event provider (returned by cclEventProviderGetID function). consumerBuffer This buffer is filled with the IDs of subscribed service consumers. bufferSize The maximum number of IDs to be written to the buffer. This parameter is updated with the actual number of consumer IDs written to the buffer.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The list of subscribed consumers was successfully read. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDEVENTPROVIDERID The eventProviderID parameter does not identify a known service event provider. CCL_BUFFERTOSMALL The specified buffer size if too small for the currently subscribed consumers.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclEventProviderGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclEventProviderID; cclEventProviderID cclEventProviderGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a a service event provider ID, which can be used in event registration functions. All event provider IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a service event provider. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the event provider. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDEVENTPROVIDERPATH The path parameter does not identify a service event provider known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclFieldGetNrOfSubscribedConsumers
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclFieldGetNrOfSubscribedConsumers(cclFieldProviderID fieldProviderID, int32_t* nrOfConsumers);
description: Description Returns the number of consumers currently subscribed at given service field provider.
parameters: Parameters fieldProviderID The ID of a service field provider (returned by cclFieldProviderGetID function). nrOfConsumers This variable returns the number of currently subscribed consumers.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The number of subscribed consumers was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDFIELDPROVIDERID The fieldProviderID parameter does not identify a known service field provider.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclFieldGetSubscribedConsumers
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclFieldGetSubscribedConsumers(cclFieldProviderID fieldProviderID, cclConsumerID* consumerBuffer, int32_t* bufferSize);
description: Description Returns a list of the consumers currently subscribed at given service field provider.
parameters: Parameters fieldProviderID The ID of a service field provider (returned by cclFieldProviderGetID function). consumerBuffer This buffer is filled with the IDs of subscribed service consumers. bufferSize The maximum number of IDs to be written to the buffer. This parameter is updated with the actual number of consumer IDs written to the buffer.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The list of subscribed consumers was successfully read. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDFIELDPROVIDERID The fieldProviderID parameter does not identify a known service field provider. CCL_BUFFERTOSMALL The specified buffer size if too small for the currently subscribed consumers.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclFieldProviderGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclFieldProviderID; cclFieldProviderID cclFieldProviderGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a service field provider ID, which can be used in field registration functions. All field provider IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a service field provider. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the field provider. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDFIELDPROVIDERPATH The path parameter does not identify a service field provider known to the system. CCL_FIELDNOTSUBSCRIBABLE The field does not have a notification and therefore cannot be used in field registration functions.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclFunctionGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32 t cclFunctionID; cclFunctionID cclFunctionGetID(cclDomain domain,const char* path);
description: Description Returns a function ID, which can be used for invocation of network function calls or simulation of network functions. All function IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement. Network functions can be free function communication objects or service methods or distributed object methods.
parameters: Parameters domain Use CCL_COMMUNICATION_OBJECTS for a communication object method and CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS for a distributed object method. path The path to a network function port or distributed object member. See Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the network function. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPATH The path parameter does not identify a network function port/distributed object member known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclFunctionSetHandler
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclFunctionSetHandler(cclFunctionID functionID, cclCallState callState, cclCallHandler handler);
description: Description Registers an event routine for a network function call state transition. The event routine is called back whenever any function call at the specified network function port changes its state.
parameters: Parameters functionID ID of the network function call port to be observed. callState The call state (enumerated value from cclCallState) for which to register the handler. When that state is entered, the handler is called back. handler The event routine to be called back. It receives the simulation time when the state transition occurred and the call context ID for accessing parameters and state of the call.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The handler was successfully registered. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONID The functionID parameter does not identify a known network function port object. CCL_PARAMETERINVALID The callState parameter must be an enumerated value from cclCallState.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclGetConsumedService
syntax: Function Syntax cclConsumedServiceID cclGetConsumedService(cclServiceID serviceID, int32_t consumerID, int32_t providerID);
description: Description Searches a consumer side service port by its binding specific endpoint identifiers.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of service communication object for which to find a consumer side port. consumerID The binding specific identifier of the consumer. providerID The binding specific identifier of the provider.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the found service port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_CONSUMERNOTFOUND No consumer with given binding identifier could be found for the service. CCL_PROVIDERNOTFOUND No provider with given binding identifier could be found for the service.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclGetConsumer
syntax: Function Syntax cclConsumerID cclGetConsumer(cclServiceID serviceID, int32_t bindingID);
description: Description Searches a service consumer by its binding specific identifier.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of service communication object for which to find a consumer. bindingID The binding specific identifier of the consumer.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the found service consumer. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_CONSUMERNOTFOUND No consumer with given binding identifier could be found for the service.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclGetProvidedService
syntax: Function Syntax cclProvidedServiceID cclGetProvidedService(cclServiceID serviceID, int32_t consumerID, int32_t providerID);
description: Description Searches a provider side service port by its binding specific endpoint identifiers.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of service communication object for which to find a provider side port. consumerID The binding specific identifier of the consumer. providerID The binding specific identifier of the provider.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the found service port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_CONSUMERNOTFOUND No consumer with given binding identifier could be found for the service. CCL_PROVIDERNOTFOUND No provider with given binding identifier could be found for the service.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclGetProvider
syntax: Function Syntax cclProviderID cclGetProvider(cclServiceID serviceID, int32_t bindingID);
description: Description Searches a service provider by its binding specific identifier.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of service communication object for which to find a provider. bindingID The binding specific identifier of the provider.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the found service provider. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_PROVIDERNOTFOUND No provider with given binding identifier could be found for the service.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclGetUserFilePath
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclGetUserFilePath(const char* filename, char* pathBuffer, int32_t pathBufferLength);
description: Description Gets the absolute path of a user file. In case of execution in a distributed environment the absolute user file path (including filename) on the remote device (e.g. VN8900 ) is returned if the user file was predefined. If the file was not predefined an error code is returned. In case of a single PC environment the registered absolute file path (including filename) of the user file is returned if the user file was predefined. If the file was not predefined the function converts a path relative to the configuration directory to an absolute path. Returns the same result as getAbsFilePath (converting a path relative to the configuration directory to an absolute path).
parameters: Parameters filename A filename potentially containing a path. If the filename is predefined the path is ignored. Otherwise the path is interpreted as relative to the current configuration path in a single PC environment. pathBuffer Buffer to which the full path name should be copied. pathBufferLength
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_USERFILENOTDEFINED The file was not predefined. CCL_INVALIDNAME Parameter filename is a null pointer or does not contain any character. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED CANoe / CANalyzer version 7.5 SP2 or newer is required for this function. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and during a running measurement. CCL_BUFFERTOSMALL The specified buffer is too small for the absolute path of the user file. CCL_PARAMETERINVALID Parameter pathBuffer is a null pointer or parameter pathBufferLength is less or equal zero.

name: cclIncrementTimerBase
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclIncrementTimerBase(int64_t newTimeBaseTicks, int32_t numberOfSteps);
description: Description Increases the simulation time by newTimeBaseTicks . Use numberOfSteps steps to reach the requested timestamp. For each step, the simulation time is increased. Events that are due are delivered and handlers are called. The function returns immediately and should not be called again unless your CANoe finished its simulation step. This function can be called from any thread.
parameters: Parameters newTimeBaseTicks The time span in nanoseconds by which the simulation time should be increased. numberOfSteps The number of steps for the increment of the simulation time.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The time span was set successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement and when your CANoe is running with simulated bus with external time source. CCL_PARAMETERINVALID Both parameters must be greater zero.
example: Example See description and example for simulated bus with external time source .

name: cclIsRunningInSlaveMode
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclIsRunningInSlaveMode(bool *isSlaveMode);
description: Description Returns whether the simulation is running with simulated bus with external time source or not. Note The slave mode in CANoe versions prior to 19 has been replaced by the direct configuration of the time source. Select External: Software to get the behavior of the slave mode, or External: SIL Kit if SIL Kit should take over the time control.
parameters: Parameters isSlaveMode Pointer to Boolean indicating if simulation is running with simulated bus with external time source.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS Always succeeds.
example: Example void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; bool isSlaveMode; rc = cclIsRunningInSlaveMode(&isSlaveMode); }

name: cclIsServiceProvided
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclIsServiceProvided(cclProviderID providerID, bool* isProvided);
description: Description Returns whether a service provider is currently accepting service requests.
parameters: Parameters providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions). isProvided This variable returns whether the service is currently provided.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The current state of the provider was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclIsServiceRequested
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclIsServiceRequested(cclConsumedServiceID consumedServiceID, bool* isRequested);
description: Description Checks whether a consumer side service port currently requests the service.
parameters: Parameters consumedServiceID The ID of a consumer side service port (returned by cclConsumedServiceGetID function). isRequested This variable returns whether the service is currently requested.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The current state of the provider side service port was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDSERVICEID The consumedServiceID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service port object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclLinChangeSchedtable
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinChangeSchedtable(int32_t channel, uint32_t tableIndex);
description: Description Switches from the current schedule table to another one.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel for which the scheduler is to be changed. tableIndex Index of the schedule table to be changed to.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement or in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinGetChannelNumber
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinGetChannelNumber(const char* networkName, int32_t* channel);
description: Description Retrieve the LIN channel number for the given network name.
parameters: Parameters networkName The name of the network, for which a LIN channel should be retrieved. Channel Pointer to a variable, where the function should store the retrieved LIN channel.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The channel was successfully retrieved. CCL_NETWORKNOTDEFINED The CANoe configuration does not contain a LIN network with the given name. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED Functionality is not implemented, version 11.0 SP3 or newer is required. CCL_INVALIDNAME The networkName parameter is a NULL pointer or an empty string.
example: Example int32_t gChannelChassis = 0; void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; rc = cclLinGetChannelNumber("Chassies", &gChannelChassis); }

name: cclLinGotoSleep
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinGotoSleep(int32_t channel);
description: Description Send a goto-sleep master request and set hardware channel into sleep mode.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel that shall be set to sleep mode.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was called successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinGotoSleepInternal
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinGotoSleepInternal(int32_t channel);
description: Description Set hardware channel into sleep mode, without sending a goto-sleep master request.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel that shall be set to sleep mode.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was called successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinSendHeader
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinSendHeader(int32_t channel, uint32_t id);
description: Description The function sends a LIN header over the specified channel.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel on which the LIN header is to be sent. id Identifier of the LIN header to be sent.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinSetErrorHandler
syntax: Function Syntax typedef enum { CCL_LIN_ERROR_CHECKSUM, CCL_LIN_ERROR_TRANSMISSION, CCL_LIN_ERROR_RECEIVE, CCL_LIN_ERROR_SYNC, CCL_LIN_ERROR_SLAVETIMEOUT } cclLinErrorType; struct cclLinError { int64_t time; int32_t channel; uint32_t id; cclLinErrorType type; uint16_t crc; uint8_t dlc; uint8_t dir; uint8_t FSMId; uint8_t FSMState; uint8_t fullTime; uint8_t headerTime; uint8_t simulated; uint8_t data[8]; uint8_t offendingByte; uint8_t shortError; uint8_t stateReason; uint8_t timeoutDuringDlcDetection; uint8_t followState; uint8_t slaveId; uint8_t stateId; }; int32_t cclLinSetErrorHandler(int32_t channel, uint32_t identifier, void (*function) (struct cclLinError* error));
description: Description Registration of an event routine for the reception of LIN errors. Registration of the handler may only be carried out in the PreStartHandler. The registered event routines are unregistered automatically at the end of measurement.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel for which the event routine is to be active. identifier LIN identifier. function The event routine to be registered.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event routine was registered successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement.
example: Example extern void OnLinErrorHandler(struct cclLinError* error); void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; rc = cclLinSetErrorHandler(1, CCL_LIN_ALLMESSAGES, &OnLinErrorHandler)); if (CCL_SUCCESS != rc) { cclWrite("C Library Example: Could not set error handler"); } } void OnLinErrorHandler(struct cclLinError* error) { cclPrintf("C Library Example: error received:"); switch (error->type) { case CCL_LIN_ERROR_CHECKSUM: cclPrintf("Checksum error, Frame-ID=%i, CRC=%i", error->id, error->crc); break; case CCL_LIN_ERROR_TRANSMISSION: cclPrintf("Transmission error, FSM-ID=%i", error->FSMId); break; case CCL_LIN_ERROR_RECEIVE: cclPrintf("Receive error, FSM-ID=%i, offending byte:%i", error->FSMId, error->offendingByte); break; case CCL_LIN_ERROR_SYNC: cclPrintf("Sync error"); break; case CCL_LIN_ERROR_SLAVETIMEOUT: cclPrintf("Slave timeout error, slave ID=%i", error->slaveId); break; default: break; } }

name: cclLinSetFrameHandler
syntax: Function Syntax struct cclLinFrame { int64_t timestampEOF; int64_t timestampSOF; int64_t timestampEOH; int64_t timeSynBreak; int64_t timeSynDel; int32_t channel; uint32_t id; uint8_t dir; uint8_t dlc; uint8_t data[8]; }; int32_t cclLinSetFrameHandler(int32_t channel, uint32_t identifier, void (*function) (struct cclLinFrame* frame) ); Struct struct cclLinFrame The struct that is transferred to the event routine, contains the following fields: CCL_DIR_RX The message was received over the bus or sent by another component in CANoe/CANalyzer . CCL_DIR_TX The message was sent by this or another C library.
description: Description Registration of an event routine for the reception of LIN frames. The handler is also called if the frame is sent with CANalyzer . Registration of the handler may only be carried out in the PreStartHandler of the measurement; the registered event routines are unregistered automatically at the end of measurement.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel for which the event routine is to be active. identifier The LIN identifier of the message for which the event routine is to be registered. The special value CCL_LIN_ALLMESSAGES registers the event routine for all messages of the specified LIN channel. function The event routine to be registered. The event routine is called as soon as an appropriate LIN frame has been received or sent.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer.

name: cclLinSetPreDispatchMessageHandler
syntax: Function Syntax struct cclLinPreDispatchMessage { int64_t time; int32_t channel; uint8_t dir; uint32_t id; uint32_t flags; uint32_t dlc; uint8_t data[8]; }; int32_t cclLinSetPreDispatchMessageHandler(int32_t channel, uint32_t identifier, void (*function) (struct cclLinPreDispatchMessage *frame));
description: Description Registration of an event routine for the reception of LIN messages before they are dispatched. Registration of the handler may only be carried out in the PreStartHandler. The registered event routines are unregistered automatically at the end of measurement.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel identifier LIN identifier of the message to be received. function The event routine to be registered.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event routine was registered successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement.
example: Example extern void OnLinPreDispatchMessageHandler(struct cclLinPreDispatchMessage* message); void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; rc = cclLinSetPreDispatchMessageHandler(1, 34, &OnLinPreDispatchMessageHandler)); if (CCL_SUCCESS != rc) { cclWrite("C Library Example: Could not set pre dispatch message handler"); } } void OnLinPreDispatchMessageHandler (struct cclLinPreDispatchMessage* message) { cclPrintf("C Library Example: pre dispatch message for ID = %i received", message->id); // Modify data of the message: if (message->dlc >= 1) // Make sure there is at least one byte in the message { message->data[0] = 0xff; // Set it to some value } }

name: cclLinSetSlaveResponseChangedHandler
syntax: Function Syntax struct cclLinSlaveResponse { int64_t time; int32_t channel; uint8_t dir; uint32_t id; uint32_t flags; uint8_t dlc; uint8_t data[8]; }; int32_t cclLinSetSlaveResponseChangedHandler(int32_t channel, uint32_t identifier, void (*function) (struct cclLinSlaveResponse* frame) );
description: Description Registration of an event routine for the reception of changed data bytes of LIN frames. Registration of the handler may only be carried out in the PreStartHandler. The registered event routines are unregistered automatically at the end of measurement.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel for which the event routine is to be active. function The event routine to be registered. The event routine is called as soon as a LIN Slave changed the data bytes of the frame.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event routine was registered successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer.
example: Example extern void OnLinSlaveResponseChangedHandler(struct cclLinSlaveResponse* frame); void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; rc = cclLinSetSlaveResponseChangedHandler(1, CCL_LIN_ALLMESSAGES, &OnLinSlaveResponseChangedHandler)); if (CCL_SUCCESS != rc) { cclWrite("C Library Example: Could not set slave response changed handler"); } } void OnLinSlaveResponseChangedHandler(struct cclLinSlaveResponse* response) { cclPrintf("C Library Example: slave response changed: %i", response->id); }

name: cclLinSetSleepModeEventHandler
syntax: Function Syntax struct cclLinSleepModeEvent { int64_t time; int32_t channel; uint8_t external; uint8_t isAwake; uint8_t reason; uint8_t wasAwake; }; int32_t cclLinSetSleepModeEventHandler(int32_t channel, void (*function) (struct cclLinSleepModeEvent *event));
description: Description Registration of an event routine for the reception of LIN sleep mode events. Registration of the handler may only be carried out in the PreStartHandler. The registered event routines are unregistered automatically at the end of measurement.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel or -1 (for all channels) function The event routine to be registered.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event routine was registered successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement.
example: Example extern void OnLinSleepModeEventHandler(struct cclLinSleepModeEvent* event); void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; rc = cclLinSetSleepModeEventHandler(1, &OnLinSleepModeEventHandler)); if (CCL_SUCCESS != rc) { cclWrite("C Library Example: Could not set sleep mode event handler"); } } void OnLinSleepModeEventHandler (struct cclLinSleepModeEvent* event) { cclPrintf("C Library Example: sleep mode event received:"); if (event->isAwake) { cclPrintf("Channel %i is NOT sleeping", event->channel); } else { cclPrintf("Channel %i is sleeping", event->channel); } }

name: cclLinSetWakeupBehavior
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinSetWakeupBehavior(int32_t channel, uint32_t restartScheduler, uint32_t wakeupIdentifier, uint32_t sendShortHeader, uint32_t wakeupDelimiterLength);
description: Description Specifies the action to take right after the hardware channel has been set into awake mode. Note : A short header (SynchBreak / SynchField) pair will be sent after the HW channel entered awake state, if wakeupIdentifier is an invalid LIN identifier AND sendShortHeader == 1 AND no master node defining schedule tables has been assigned to it's corresponding LIN DB node.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel restartScheduler Restart scheduler after HW channel entered awake state. wakeupIdentifier LIN frame identifier to be sent additionally directly after sending a wakeup signal. sendShortHeader Activates/deactivates short header automatism (use 1 for default behavior as in CANoe). wakeupDelimiterLength Time, after wakeup, when to send the first header, if restartScheduler == 1 (use 0 for default value).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was called successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinSetWakeupCondition
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinSetWakeupCondition(int32_t channel, uint32_t numWakeupSignals, uint32_t forgetWupsAfterMS);
description: Description Sets wakeup signal parameters, defining the condition when to actually set the hardware channel into awake mode.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel numWakeupSignals Number of wakeup signals received, after which to set the hardware channel into awake mode. forgetWupsAfterMS Time span after which the count of received wakeup signals will be reset (while not causing a HW channel wakeup).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was called successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinSetWakeupFrameHandler
syntax: Function Syntax struct cclLinWakeupFrame { int64_t time; int32_t channel; uint8_t external; uint8_t signal; }; int32_t cclLinSetWakeupFrameHandler(int32_t channel, void (*function) (struct cclLinWakeupFrame *frame));
description: Description Registration of an event routine for the reception of LIN wakeup frames. Registration of the handler may only be carried out in the PreStartHandler. The registered event routines are unregistered automatically at the end of measurement.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel or -1 (for all channels) function The event routine to be registered.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event routine was registered successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The parameter function is a ZERO pointer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up in the PreStartHandler of the measurement.
