example: Example extern void OnLinWakeupFrameHandler(struct cclLinWakeupFrame* frame); void OnMeasurementPreStart() { int32_t rc; rc = cclLinSetWakeupFrameHandler(1, &OnLinWakeupFrameHandler)); if (CCL_SUCCESS != rc) { cclWrite("C Library Example: Could not set wakeup frame handler"); } } void OnLinWakeupFrameHandler (struct cclLinWakeupFrame* frame) { cclPrintf("C Library Example: wakeup frame received"); }

name: cclLinSetWakeupTimings
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinSetWakeupTimings(int32_t channel, uint32_t ttobrk, uint32_t wakeupSignalCount, uint32_t widthinMicSec);
description: Description Sets timing parameters, on the current node, for consecutive LINWakeup invocation.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel ttobrk Time difference (bit times) between two consecutive frames. wakeupSignalCount The number of Wakeup frames to be sent. widthinMicSec The length of each wakeup signal, in microseconds.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was called successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinStartScheduler
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinStartScheduler(int32_t channel);
description: Description This function starts the internal scheduler, which begins a cyclical traversal of a last con-figured schedule table.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel for which the scheduler is to be started.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement or in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinStopScheduler
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinStopScheduler(int32_t channel);
description: Description This function stops the internal scheduler. This way a cyclical traversal of the current schedule table is stopped.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel for which the scheduler is to be stopped.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement or in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinUpdateResponseData
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinUpdateResponseData(int32_t channel,uint32_t id,uint8_t dlc,uint8_t data[8]);
description: Description This function sets the response data for an LIN frame.
parameters: Parameters channel LIN channel for which the response data is to be changed. id Identifier of the LIN frame for which the response data is to be changed. data length code Length of the response in bytes. data Data bytes.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement or in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinWakeup
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinWakeup(int32_t channel);
description: Description Send wakeup master signal(s) and set hardware channel into awake mode. Node-specific timing parameters will be used if available, default parameters based on protocol version and type otherwise.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel that shall be set to awake mode.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was called successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclLinWakeupInternal
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclLinWakeupInternal(int32_t channel);
description: Description Sets hardware channel into awake mode, without sending wakeup signals.
parameters: Parameters channel The LIN channel that shall be set to awake mode.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function was called successfully. CCL_INVALIDCHANNEL The specified LIN channel is invalid.

name: cclOnDllLoad
syntax: Function Syntax void cclOnDllLoad();
description: Description This function is the entry function of a C library and must be implemented by you. It is called up by the framework shortly after the library was loaded by your CANoe . The function is used to initialize the library and to register additional event routines. The following functions are typically used within cclOnLoad : cclSetMeasurementPreStartHandler cclSetMeasurementStartHandler cclSetMea-surementStopHandler cclsetDllUnloadHandler
parameters: Parameters —
returnValues: Return Values —

name: cclPDUGetNrOfSubscribedConsumers
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclPDUGetNrOfSubscribedConsumers(cclPDUProviderID pduProviderID, int32_t* nrOfConsumers);
description: Description Returns the number of consumers currently subscribed at given service PDU provider.
parameters: Parameters pduProviderID The ID of a service PDU provider (returned by cclPDUProviderGetID function). nrOfConsumers This variable returns the number of currently subscribed consumers.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The number of subscribed consumers was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPDUPROVIDERID The pduProviderID parameter does not identify a known service PDU provider.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclPDUGetSubscribedConsumers
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclPDUGetSubscribedConsumers(cclPDUProviderID pduProviderID, cclConsumerID* consumerBuffer, int32_t* bufferSize);
description: Description Returns a list of the consumers currently subscribed at given service PDU provider.
parameters: Parameters pduProviderID The ID of a service PDU provider (returned by cclPDUProviderGetID function). consumerBuffer This buffer is filled with the IDs of subscribed service consumers. bufferSize The maximum number of IDs to be written to the buffer. This parameter is updated with the actual number of consumer IDs written to the buffer.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The list of subscribed consumers was successfully read. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPDUPROVIDERID The pduProviderID parameter does not identify a known service PDU provider. CCL_BUFFERTOSMALL The specified buffer size if too small for the currently subscribed consumers.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclPDUProviderGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclPDUProviderID; cclPDUProviderID cclPDUProviderGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a service PDU provider ID, which can be used for accessing PDU signal values and in PDU registration functions. All service PDU provider IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a service PDU provider. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the service PDU provider. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPDUPROVIDERPATH The path parameter does not identify a service PDU provider known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclPDUProviderSignalValueGetID
syntax: Function Syntax cclValueID cclPDUProviderSignalValueGetID(cclPDUProviderID pduProviderID, const char* signal, const char* member, cclValueRepresentation repr);
description: Description Returns a value ID for a signal in a service PDU provider, which can be used for reading and setting signal values. All PDU signal value IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters pduProviderID The ID of service PDU provider for which to access a signal value. signal The name of the signal in the PDU. member A member path for addressing a sub-value of the signal in case it has a complex data type. You may pass literal nullptr to get access to the whole signal value. repr The representation parameter can take the values CCL_IMPL, CCL_RAW or CCL_PHYS for access to the application implementation level, network transport or physical representation of the value.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the signal value. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPDUPROVIDERID The pduProviderID parameter does not identify a known service PDU provider. CCL_INVALIDSIGNALNAME The signal parameter does not address a known signal of the PDU. CCL_INVALIDMEMBERPATH The member parameter does not address a valid sub-value of the signal.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclPDUReceiverGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclPDUReceiverID; cclPDUReceiverID cclPDUReceiverGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a receiver side PDU port ID, which can be used for accessing PDU signal values. All PDU port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a receiver side PDU port. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the PDU receiver port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPDURECEIVERPATH The path parameter does not identify a PDU receiver port known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclPDUReceiverSignalValueGetID
syntax: Function Syntax cclValueID cclPDUReceiverSignalValueGetID(cclPDUReceiverID pduReceiverID, const char* signal, const char* member, cclValueRepresentation repr);
description: Description Returns a value ID for a signal in a receiver side PDU port, which can be used for reading signal values. All PDU signal value IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters pduReceiverID The ID of receiver side PDU port for which to access a signal value. signal The name of the signal in the PDU. member A member path for addressing a sub-value of the signal in case it has a complex data type. You may pass literal nullptr to get access to the whole signal value. repr The representation parameter can take the values CCL_IMPL, CCL_RAW or CCL_PHYS for access to the application implementation level, network transport or physical representation of the value.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the signal value. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPDURECEIVERID The pduReceiverID parameter does not identify a known PDU receiver port. CCL_INVALIDSIGNALNAME The signal parameter does not address a kown signal of the PDU. CCL_INVALIDMEMBERPATH The member parameter does not address a valid sub-value of the signal.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclPDUSenderGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclPDUSenderID; cclPDUSenderID cclPDUSenderGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a sender side PDU port ID, which can be used for accessing PDU signal values. All PDU port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a sender side PDU port. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the PDU sender port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPDUSENDERPATH The path parameter does not identify a PDU sender port known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclPDUSenderSignalValueGetID
syntax: Function Syntax cclValueID cclPDUSenderSignalValueGetID(cclPDUSenderID pduSenderID, const char* signal, const char* member, cclValueRepresentation repr);
description: Description Returns a value ID for a signal in a sender side PDU port, which can be used for reading and setting signal values. All PDU signal value IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters pduSenderID The ID of sender side PDU port for which to access a signal value. signal The name of the signal in the PDU. member A member path for addressing a sub-value of the signal in case it has a complex data type. You may pass literal nullptr to get access to the whole signal value. repr The representation parameter can take the values CCL_IMPL, CCL_RAW or CCL_PHYS for access to the application implementation level, network transport or physical representation of the value.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the signal value. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPDUSENDERID The pduSenderID parameter does not identify a known PDU sender port. CCL_INVALIDSIGNALNAME The signal parameter does not address a kown signal of the PDU. CCL_INVALIDMEMBERPATH The member parameter does not address a valid sub-value of the signal.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclPrintf
syntax: Function Syntax void cclPrintf(const char* format, ...);
description: Description Print formatted output to the Write Window of your CANoe . The function uses the printf function of the standard C library of your development environment to format the output. For details about the format string look at the description of printf in the help of your development environment.
parameters: Parameters format The format string, which controls the output of the text.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: cclProvidedEventGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclProvidedEventID; cclProvidedEventID cclProvidedEventGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a provider side service event port ID, which can be used in event registration functions. All event port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a provider side service event port. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the event port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDEVENTPATH The path parameter does not identify a provider side service event port known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclProvidedEventSetSubscriptionStateIsolated
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclProvidedEventSetSubscriptionStateIsolated(cclProvidedEventID providedEventID, bool subscribed);
description: Description Sets the low level subscription state of a provider side service event port.
parameters: Parameters providedEventID The ID of a provider side service event port (returned by cclProvidedEventGetID function). subscribed If this parameter is set to true, the event port will transition to state CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_SUBSCRIBED, otherwise it will transition back to CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_AVAILABLE.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The subscription state was successfully changed. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDEVENTID The providedEventID parameter does not identify a known provider side service event port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclProvidedFieldGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclProvidedFieldID; cclProvidedFieldID cclProvidedFieldGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a provider side service field port ID, which can be used in field registration functions. All field port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a provider side service field port. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the field port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDFIELDPATH The path parameter does not identify a provider side service field port known to the system. CCL_FIELDNOTSUBSCRIBABLE The field does not have a notification and therefore cannot be used in field registration functions.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclProvidedFieldSetSubscriptionStateIsolated
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclProvidedFieldSetSubscriptionStateIsolated(cclProvidedFieldID providedFieldID, bool subscribed);
description: Description Sets the low level subscription state of a provider side service field port.
parameters: Parameters providedFieldID The ID of a provider side service field port (returned by cclProvidedFieldGetID function). subscribed If this parameter is set to true, the field port will transition to state CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_SUBSCRIBED, otherwise it will transition back to CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_AVAILABLE.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The subscription state was successfully changed. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDFIELDID The providedFieldID parameter does not identify a known provider side service field port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclProvidedPDUGetID
syntax: Function Syntax cclPDUSenderID cclProvidedPDUGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a provider side service PDU port ID, which can be used for accessing PDU signal values and in PDU registration functions. Provider side service PDU port IDs can be used in place of sender side non-service PDU port IDs but not vice-versa. All PDU port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a provider side service PDU port. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the PDU port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDPDUPATH The path parameter does not identify a provider side service PDU port known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclProvidedPDUSetSubscriptionStateIsolated
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclProvidedPDUSetSubscriptionStateIsolated(cclPDUSenderID providedPDUID, bool subscribed);
description: Description Sets the low level subscription state of a provider side service PDU port.
parameters: Parameters providedPDUID The ID of a provider side service PDU port (returned by cclProvidedPDUGetID function). subscribed If this parameter is set to true, the PDU port will transition to state CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_SUBSCRIBED, otherwise it will transition back to CCL_SUBSCRIPTION_STATE_AVAILABLE.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The subscription state was successfully changed. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDPDUID The providedPDUID parameter does not identify a known provider side service PDU port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclProvidedServiceGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclProvidedServiceID; cclProvidedServiceID cclProvidedServiceGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a provider side service port ID, which can be used in service discovery functions. All service port IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a provider side service port. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the provider side service port. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEPATH The path parameter does not identify a provider side service port known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclProvidedServiceValueGetID
syntax: Function Syntax cclValueID cclProvidedServiceValueGetID(cclProvidedServiceID providedServiceID, const char* member, cclValueRepresentation repr);
description: Description Returns the communication object value identifier for a provider side service port. The function is a convenience shortcut for accessing service port parameters when the service port is already available and its path is unknown.
parameters: Parameters providedServiceID The ID of provider side service port for which to access a value (e.g. connection state). member A member path for addressing a specific value of the port (CCL_MEMBER_CONNECTION_STATE or CCL_MEMBER_SERVICE_STATE for provider side service ports). repr The representation parameter can take the values CCL_IMPL, CCL_RAW or CCL_PHYS for access to the application implementation level, network transport or physical representation of the value.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the value. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDSERVICEID The providedServiceID parameter does not identify a known provider side service port object. CCL_INVALIDMEMBERPATH The member path does not identify a valid value of the service port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclProviderGetBindingID
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclProviderGetBindingID(cclProviderID providerID, int32_t* bindingID);
description: Description Returns a binding specific identifier for a service provider, which can be used to access ports of dynamically added providers.
parameters: Parameters providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions). bindingID The binding specific identifier is written to this variable.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The binding ID was successfully determined. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object. CCL_PROVIDERNOTBOUND The provider must be bound to an address.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclProviderGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclProviderID; cclProviderID cclProviderGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a provider side service endpoint ID, which can be used in service discovery functions. All provider IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a service provider. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the service provider. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEPATH The path parameter does not identify a service provider known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclProvideService
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclProvideService(cclProviderID providerID);
description: Description Switches a service provider into service state CCL_SERVICE_STATE_AVAILABLE for sending out service announcements and responding to service requests by consumers.
parameters: Parameters providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The service is successfully provided. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclReleaseService
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclReleaseService(cclProviderID providerID);
description: Description Switches a service provider back to service state CCL_SERVICE_STATE_UNAVAILABLE, which makes it stop sending service announcements and accepting service requests.
parameters: Parameters providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS Providing the service was successfully stopped. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclReleaseServiceRequest
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclReleaseServiceRequest(cclConsumedServiceID consumedServiceID);
description: Description Stops requesting the service from a consumer side service port. If currently connected, the connection will be released as well.
parameters: Parameters consumedServiceID The ID of a consumer side service port (returned by cclConsumedServiceGetID function).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The port no more requests the service and any connection is released. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The consumedServiceID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service port object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclRemoveConsumer
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclRemoveConsumer(cclServiceID serviceID, cclConsumerID consumerID);
description: Description Removes a dynamically added consumer side endpoint from a service.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of service communication object from which to remove a dynamic consumer. consumerID ID of the consumer to be removed (returned by cclAddConsumerByName or cclAddConsumerByAddress ).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The consumer was successfully removed from the service. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMERID The consumerID parameter does not identify a known service consumer object. CCL_STATICCONSUMER The identified consumer was not dynamically added to the service and thus cannot be removed.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclRemoveProvider
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclRemoveProvider(cclServiceID serviceID, cclProviderID providerID);
description: Description Removes a dynamically added provider side endpoint from a service.
parameters: Parameters serviceID The ID of service communication object from which to remove a dynamic provider. providerID ID of the provider to be removed (returned by cclAddProviderByName or cclAddProviderByAddress ).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The provider was successfully removed from the service. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEID The serviceID parameter does not identify a known service communication object. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object. CCL_STATICPROVIDER The identified provider was not dynamically added to the service and thus cannot be removed.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclRequestService
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclRequestService(cclConsumedServiceID consumedServiceID);
description: Description Switches a consumer side service port to start requesting the service. As long as the consumer side service port is in that state, it will try to connect to the service provider.
parameters: Parameters consumedServiceID The ID of a consumer side service port (returned by cclConsumedServiceGetID functions).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The port now requests the service. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDSERVICEID The consumedServiceID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service port object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclServiceGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclServiceID; cclServiceID cclServiceGetID(const char* path);
description: Description Returns a service communication object ID, which can be used for service method simulation and dynamic endpoint discovery. All service IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters path The path to a service communication object. See also Communication Object Paths .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the service. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSERVICEPATH The path parameter does not identify a service communication object known to the system.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclServiceSetSimulator
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32_t cclProvidedServiceID; int32_t cclServiceSetSimulator(cclProvidedServiceID providedService, cclCallHandler simulator);
description: Description Registers a service method simulation event routine for all methods of a provider side service port.
parameters: Parameters providedService ID of the provider side service port. simulator The event routine to be called back when any method of the service transitions into state called at provider side. The event routine receives the simulation time when the state transition occurred and the call context ID for accessing parameters and state of the call.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The network function port path was successfully written to the buffer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDEDSERVICEID The providedService parameter does not identify a known provider side service port.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COFunction.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSetConnectionRequestedHandler
syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclConnectionRequestedHandler)(cclConsumerID consumerID); int32_t cclSetConnectionRequestedHandler(cclProviderID providerID, cclConnectionRequestedHandler handler);
description: Description Registers an event routine to be called back when a service consumer requests a service connection.
parameters: Parameters providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions) for which to register the handler. handler Event routine to be called back when a service consumer requests a service connection.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The handler was successfully registered. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSetConsumerAddress
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSetConsumerAddress(cclConsumerID consumerID, cclAddressID addressID, cclProviderID providerID);
description: Description Notifies the system about a service consumer's binding specific address. The function can either notify the service consumer about its own address or notify a service provider about the consumer's address.
parameters: Parameters consumerID The ID of a service consumer (returned by cclAddConsumerByAddress , cclAddConsumerByName and cclConsumerGetID functions) for which to set given address. addressID The ID of a binding specific service consumer address. providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions) to be notified about the consumer's address. The constant CCL_PROVIDER_NONE should be used for notifying the consumer about its own address.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The service was successfully announced to the consumer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMERID The consumerID parameter does not identify a known service consumer object. CCL_INVALIDADDRESSID The addressID parameter does not identify a known address object. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclSetDllUnloadHandler
syntax: Function Syntax void cclSetDllUnloadHandler (void (*function)());
description: Description This function registers an event routine with the framework. The transferring event routine is called up by the framework shortly before the library is cleared by your CANoe .
parameters: Parameters function Event routine that is called up by the framework shortly before the DLL is cleared.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: cclSetGpbOSI3Data
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSetGpbOSI3Data (uint8_t * data, int32_t dataSize)
description: Description Passes OSI3 sensor data which are serialized as Google Protocol Buffers (GPB) to CANoe. CANoe will generate Distributed Objects (DOs) based on the sensor data.
parameters: Parameters data The byte array containing the serialized OSI3 sensor data. dataSize The length of the array in byte.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The functional call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDDATA The provided data can't be decoded. The byte array either isn't a Google Protocol Buffer or isn't a in a compatible OSI3 sensor data format.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ADAS.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples\ADAS).

name: cclSetMeasurementPreStartHandler
syntax: Function Syntax void cclSetMeasurementPreStartHandler(void (*function)());
description: Description This function registers an event routine with the framework. The transferring event routine is called up during the initialization of a measurement by your CANoe . The measurement has not yet been started at this time, which is why longer-lasting tasks, e.g. file access, may also be carried out in this routine. Typical tasks of the event routine are the creation of timer objects, the determination of system variable IDs the registration of event routines for the reception of bus messages. Since the measurement is not yet running when the event routine is called up, messages cannot yet be output by it over the bus. Even the setting of a timer is not yet permitted.
parameters: Parameters function Event routine that is called up by the framework shortly before a measurement is started.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: cclSetMeasurementStartHandler
syntax: Function Syntax void cclSetMeasurementStartHandler(void (*function)());
description: Description This function registers the event routine with the framework. The transferring event routine is called up directly after a measurement start in your CANoe . A typical task of the transferring event routine is the starting of timers. Since the measurement is already running when the event routine is called up, the event routine should not execute any longer-lasting tasks.
parameters: Parameters function Event routine that is called up by the framework directly after a measurement is started.
returnValues: Return Values —

name: cclSetMeasurementStopHandler
syntax: Function Syntax void cclSetMeasurementStopHandler(void (*function)());
description: Description This function registers an event routine with the framework. The transferring event routine is called up directly after a measurement stop in your CANoe . The measurement has already stopped by this time, i.e. no further messages can be output over the buses.
parameters: Parameters function Event routine that is called up by the framework directly after a measurement is stopped.
returnValues: Return Values —

The constant CCL_CONSUMER_NONE should be used for notifying the provider about its own address.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The service was successfully announced to the consumer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object. CCL_INVALIDADDRESSID The addressID parameter does not identify a known address object. name: cclSignalGetID
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSignalGetID(char* signalname);
description: Description This function returns a signal ID. This ID is required for setting and reading signal values. All system variable IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters signalname The name of the signal. The identification string of a signal has the following form: [[[[network_name::]db_name::]node_name::]msg_name::]signal_name The field network_name is the name of network in the Simulation Setup of CANoe . It is only required if a communication description is assigned to more than one bus. If you are working with a communication description, where the signals are directly assigned to communication frames, the field msg_name is the name of the frame. In the case, where the signals are assigned to protocol data units (PDU), the field msg_name is the name of the PDU. The field node_name is the name of the sender of the frame or PDU (Tx Node). The node_name is only required, if you use a protocol, where a frame can have more than one sender (The protocol J1939 for example). In all other cases, the node_name can be an empty string, but the colons (::) must be added to separate it from the field db_name . Examples: "Signal_A1" // Signal "PDU_A::Signal_A2 " // PDU + Signal "ECU_B::PDU_B::Signal_B2 " // Node + PDU + Signal "Example_DB::ECU_B::PDU_B::Signal_B2 " // Database + Node + PDU + Signal "X_Bus::Example_DB::ECU_B::PDU_B::Signal_B2 " //
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the signal. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_INVALIDNAME Parameter signalname is a null pointer or does not contain any character. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED CANoe / CANalyzer version 8.1 or newer is required for this function. CCL_SIGNALNOTDEFINED The signal does not exists. CCL_SIGNALAMBIGUOUS The given signal name is ambiguous.

name: cclSignalGetRxPhysDouble
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSignalGetRxPhysDouble(int32_t signalID, double* value);
description: Description Reads the Rx value as physical value of the given signal.
parameters: Parameters signalID ID of the signal (return value of the function cclSignalGetID ). value The physical value of the signal is written to this variable.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSIGNALID The ID of the signal is invalid. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED The signal has no physical value.

name: cclSignalSetHandler
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSignalSetHandler(int32_t signalID, void (*function)(int32_t signalID) );
description: Description The function assigns an event routine to the specified signal. The event routine is called each time a frame with the given signal was transmitted on the bus.
parameters: Parameters signalID ID of the signal (return value of the function cclSignalGetID ). function The event routine to be called up when the system variable is set. The event routine receives the current time of the measurement in nanoseconds and the ID of the system variable.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The function call was successful. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSIGNALID The ID of the signal is invalid.

CCL_INVALIDSIGNALID The ID of the signal is invalid. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED The signal is read only or has no physical value.

syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSomeIPConsumedEventGroupGetEvents(cclSomeIPConsumedEventGroupID consumedEventGroupID, cclConsumedEventID* buffer, int32_t* bufferSize);
description: Description Returns the event port IDs contained in a consumer side service event group port.
parameters: Parameters consumedEventGroupID The ID of a consumer side service event group port with SOME/IP binding (returned by cclSomeIPConsumedEventGroupGetID function). buffer A buffer to be filled with service event IDs contained in the event group. bufferSize The maximum number of service event port IDs to write to the buffer. This parameter is updated with the actual number of event port IDs written to the buffer.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The service event port IDs were successfully written to the buffer. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDCONSUMEDEVENTGROUPID The consumedEventGroupID parameter does not identify a known consumer side service event group port.

name: cclSysVarGetArraySize
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSysVarGetArraySize(int32_t sysVarID);
description: Description This function returns the field length of the specified system variables. The variable must be of the integer array or float array type.
parameters: Parameters sysVarID ID of the system variable (return value of the function cclSysVarGetID ).
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the length of the field. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSYSVARID The ID of the system variable is invalid. CCL_WRONGTYPE The system variable is no Array type. CCL_SYSVARNOTDEFINED The system variable was deleted in the meantime.

name: cclSysVarGetData
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSysVarGetData (int32_t sysVarID, uint8_t* X, int32_t length);
description: Description Reads the value of a system variable of Data or Struct type.
parameters: Parameters sysVarID ID of the system variable (return value of the function cclSysVarGetID ). x The value of the system variable is written to this field variable. length Size of the field variable x .
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which is the number of bytes copied to the field variable x. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSYSVARID The ID of the system variable is invalid. CCL_WRONGTYPE The system variable is no Data type. CCL_SYSVARNOTDEFINED The system variable was deleted in the meantime. CCL_BUFFERTOSMALL The specified buffer is too small for the contents of the system variable. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED Version 7.6 or newer is required for this function.

CCL_INVALIDSYSVARID The ID of the system variable is invalid. CCL_WRONGTYPE The system variable is no Float type. CCL_SYSVARNOTDEFINED The system variable was deleted in the meantime.

name: cclSysVarGetID
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSysVarGetID(char* systemVariableName);
description: Description This function returns a system variable ID. This ID is required for setting and reading system variables. All system variable IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement.
parameters: Parameters systemVariableName The name of the system variable. In case you want to access a member of a structure, you must extend the name of the system variable by the name of the member and separate them by a dot. In case you want to access an element of an array, you must extend the name of the system variable by the index of the array element. The index must be enclosed in brackets. The first array element has the index zero. Examples: "X::a" System variable with name 'a' in namespace 'X' "Y::b.c" Member 'c' in system variable 'b' in namespace 'Y' "Y::b.d[2]" Third array element in struct member 'd' in variable 'b' in namespace 'Y'
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the system variable. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDNAME The parameter systemVariableName may be neither a zero pointer nor an empty string. CCL_SYSVARNOTDEFINED A system variable with the specified name is not defined in your CANoe .

name: cclSysVarGetPhysical
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSysVarGetPhysical(int32_t sysVarID, double* x);
description: Description Reads the physical value of a struct member or array element of a system variable.
parameters: Parameters sysVarID ID of the system variable (return value of the function cclSysVarGetID ). x The value of the system variable is written to this variable.

CCL_WRONGTYPE The struct member or array element is of wrong type. (The type must be float or integer). CCL_INVALIDSYSVARID The ID of the system variable is invalid. CCL_SYSVARNOTDEFINED The system variable was deleted in the meantime. CCL_SYSVARISREADONLY The system variable is write-protected. CCL_NOTIMPLEMENTED The function is currently only implemented for struct members and array elements.

name: cclSysVarGetType
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSysVarGetType(int32_t sysVarID);
description: Description Returns the type of a system variable. In case the sysVarID identifies only a member of a system variable struct, then the type of this member is returned. In case the sysVarID identifies only an element of an array, then the type of this array element is returned.
parameters: Parameters sysVarID ID of the system variable (return value of the function cclSysVarGetID ).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SYSVAR_INTEGER System variable, struct member or array element is of type Integer. CCL_SYSVAR_FLOAT System variable, struct member or array element is of type Float. CCL_SYSVAR_STRING System variable, struct member or array element is of type String. CCL_SYSVAR_DATA System variable, struct member or array element is of type Data. CCL_SYSVAR_INTEGERARRAY System variable, struct member or array element is of type Integer Array. CCL_SYSVAR_FLOATARRAY System variable, struct member or array element is of type Float Array CCL_SYSVAR_STRUCT System variable, struct member or array element is of type Struct. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSYSVARID The ID of the system variable is invalid. CCL_SYSVARNOTDEFINED The system variable was deleted in the meantime. CCL_SYSVAR_GENERICARRAY System variable or struct member is of type Generic Array.

name: cclSysVarSetHandler
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSysVarSetHandler(int32_t sysVarID, void (*function)(int64_t time, int32_t sysVarID) );
description: Description The function assigns an event routine to the specified system variable. The event routine is called each time the value of the variable is reset.
parameters: Parameters sysVarID ID of the system variable (return value of the function cclSysVarGetID ). function The event routine to be called up when the system variable is set. The event routine receives the current time of the measurement in nanoseconds and the ID of the system variable.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The event routine was successfully assigned to the system variable. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSYSVARID The ID of the system variable is invalid.

syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclSysVarSetInteger(int32_t sysVarID, int32_t x);
description: Description Sets the value of a system variable of Integer type.
parameters: Parameters sysVarID ID of the system variable (return value of the function cclSysVarGetID ). x The value to be assigned to the system variables.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The system variable was successfully set to the new value. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDSYSVARID The ID of the system variable is invalid. CCL_WRONGTYPE The system variable is no Integer type. CCL_SYSVARNOTDEFINED The system variable was deleted in the meantime. CCL_SYSVARISREADONLY The system variable is write-protected.

name: cclTimerCancel
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclTimerCancel(int32_t timerID);
description: Description Stops the active timer.
parameters: Parameters timerID Timer ID (return value of function cclTimerCreate ).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The timer was successfully stopped or the timer was already inactive. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDTIMERID The transferred TimerID is invalid.

name: cclTimerCreate
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclTimerCreate( void (*function)(int64_t time, int32_t timerID) );
description: Description This function creates a new timer and links the transferred event routine to the timer. Each time the timer expires, the assigned event routine is called. The created timer is activated with the function cclTimerSet and can be ended prematurely with the function cclTimerCancel . The timers required for a measurement are typically created in the PreStartHandler of the measurement (see cclSetMeasurementPreStartHandler ). The created timer is automatically deleted from the framework at the end of measurement.
parameters: Parameters function The event routine to be called up when the timer expires. The event routine receives the current time of the measurement in nanoseconds and the ID of the expired timer as parameters.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the timer. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function cclTimerCreate may only be called up during a running measurement or in the PreStartHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDFUNCTIONPOINTER The function parameter is zero.

name: cclTimerSet
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclTimerSet(int32_t timerID, int64_t nanoseconds);
description: Description The timer is set with the specified time. After the time expires, the event routine assigned to the timer is called and the timer then becomes inactive.
parameters: Parameters timerID Timer ID (return value of the function cclTimerCreate ). nanoseconds The time span in nanoseconds which the timer is to run. Only positive time specifications are permissible.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The timer was set successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called up during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDTIMERID The transferred TimerID is invalid. CCL_INVALIDTIME The transferred time span is less than or equal to zero.

name: cclTimeSeconds
syntax: Function Syntax extern int64_t cclTimeSeconds(int64_t seconds); extern int64_t cclTimeMilliseconds(int64_t milliseconds); extern int64_t cclTimeMicroseconds(int64_t microseconds);
description: Description All three functions convert the transferred time into nanoseconds. Time stamps which are transferred from the framework to the C library typically have the nanosecond unit. The function for setting a timer ( cclTimerSet ) also requires the time specification in nanoseconds.
parameters: Parameters seconds Time in seconds. milliseconds Time in milliseconds. microseconds Time in microseconds.
returnValues: Return Values Time in nanoseconds.

name: cclUnannounceProvider
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclUnannounceProvider(cclProviderID providerID);
description: Description Stops sending service announcements from a service provider.
parameters: Parameters providerID The ID of a service provider (returned by cclAddProviderByAddress , cclAddProviderByName and cclProviderGetID functions).
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS Service announcement was successfully stopped. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement. CCL_INVALIDPROVIDERID The providerID parameter does not identify a known service provider object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file ServiceDiscovery.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclValueBeginBulkUpdate
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclValueBeginBulkUpdate(cclValueID valueID);
description: Description Begins a bulk update of a complex value of a distributed object. Use this if you need to update several elements of a value with a complex data type (e.g., a struct or an array) to improve the performance. Each call to cclValueBeginBulkUpdate must be followed by a call to cclValueEndBulkUpdate . This function can not be used for communication object values or call context values.
parameters: Parameters valueID ID of the (complex) value, returned by cclValueGetID .
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS No error CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDVALUEID The valueID parameter does not identify a known value object. CCL_INVALIDDOMAIN The valueID parameter does not identify a distributed object value. CCL_VALUEREADONLY The valueID parameter identifies a value which is read-only.
example: Example cclValueID completeValue, member1, member2; completeValue = cclValueGetID(cclDomain::CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS, "MyNamespace::MyDO.MyComplexMember", 0, cclValueRepresentation::CCL_IMPL); member1 = cclValueGetID(cclDomain::CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS, "MyNamespace::MyDO.MyComplexMember", "member1", cclValueRepresentation::CCL_IMPL); member2 = cclValueGetID(cclDomain::CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS, "MyNamespace::MyDO.MyComplexMember", "member2", cclValueRepresentation::CCL_IMPL); cclValueBeginBulkUpdate(completeValue); cclValueSetInteger(member1, 1); cclValueSetInteger(member2, 4); cclValueEndBulkUpdate(completeValue);

name: cclValueClear
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclValueClear(cclValueID valueID);
description: Description Invalidates a communication object value, i.e. sets an optional value to the invalid state.
parameters: Parameters valueID ID of the value, returned by cclValueGetID cclCallContextValueGetID cclConsumedServiceValueGetID cclProvidedServiceValueGetID cclPDUSenderSignalValueGetID cclPDUReceiverSignalValueGetID cclPDUProviderSignalValueGetID
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The value was read out successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDVALUEID The valueID parameter does not identify a known value object. CCL_VALUENOTOPTIONAL The value is not optional and therefore cannot be invalidated.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COSignal.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclValueEndBulkUpdate
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclValueEndBulkUpdate(cclValueID valueID);
description: Description Ends a bulk update of a complex value of a distributed object. Use this if you need to update several elements of a value with a complex data type (e.g., a struct or an array) to improve the performance. Each call to cclValueBeginBulkUpdate must be followed by a call to cclValueEndBulkUpdate . This function can not be used for communication object values or call context values.
parameters: Parameters valueID ID of the (complex) value, returned by cclValueGetID .
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS No error CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDVALUEID The valueID parameter does not identify a known value object. CCL_INVALIDDOMAIN The valueID parameter does not identify a distributed object value. CCL_VALUEREADONLY The valueID parameter identifies a value which is read-only.
example: Example cclValueID completeValue, member1, member2; completeValue = cclValueGetID(cclDomain::CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS, "MyNamespace::MyDO.MyComplexMember", 0, cclValueRepresentation::CCL_IMPL); member1 = cclValueGetID(cclDomain::CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS, "MyNamespace::MyDO.MyComplexMember", "member1", cclValueRepresentation::CCL_IMPL); member2 = cclValueGetID(cclDomain::CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS, "MyNamespace::MyDO.MyComplexMember", "member2", cclValueRepresentation::CCL_IMPL); cclValueBeginBulkUpdate(completeValue); cclValueSetInteger(member1, 1); cclValueSetInteger(member2, 4); cclValueEndBulkUpdate(completeValue);

description: Description Reads a communication object value of Data or complex type.
parameters: Parameters valueID ID of the value, returned by cclValueGetID cclCallContextValueGetID cclConsumedServiceValueGetID cclProvidedServiceValueGetID cclPDUSenderSignalValueGetID cclPDUReceiverSignalValueGetID cclPDUProviderSignalValueGetID buffer The value is written into this buffer in its native memory layout. If the layout is not fix, the resulting data can only be used to set a value of the same type. bufferSize The number of bytes allocated for the buffer. The parameter returns the number of bytes actually used for copying the value.

name: cclValueGetID
syntax: Function Syntax typedef int32 t cclValueID; cclValueID cclValueGetID(cclDomain domain, const char* path, const char* member, cclValueRepresentation repr);
description: Description Returns a value ID of a communication object or distributed object, which can be used for setting and reading values. All value IDs are invalid at the end of the measurement, i.e. the IDs must be re-determined at the start of each measurement. Communication object values can be signal ports, service state, connection state, latest function call or return parameters, PDU sender and receiver values, etc. Distributed object values can be member values, subscription state, announcement state, latest function call or return parameters, etc.
parameters: Parameters domain Use CCL_COMMUNICATION_OBJECTS for a communication object value and CCL_DISTRIBUTED_OBJECTS for a distributed object value. path The path to a communication object port or endpoint or to a distributed object member. See Communication Object Paths . member The member identifies a sub-value of a complex type value or a specific value associated with the object. There are predefined constants CCL_MEMBER_... which (depending on the addressed value) can be concatenated with further sub-member paths. The parameter can be the literal nullptr if the object's value is directly addressed as a whole. repr The representation parameter can take the values CCL_IMPL, CCL_RAW or CCL_PHYS for access to the application implementation level, network transport or physical representation of the value.
returnValues: Return Values If successful, the function returns a value greater than or equal to 0, which represents the ID of the value. In the event of an error, the function returns a negative value: CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDDOMAIN The domain parameter must be one of the enumerated values in cclDomain. CCL_INVALIDPORTPATH The path parameter does not identify a port or endpoint known to the system. CCL_PARAMETERINVALID The repr parameter must be one of the enumerated values in cclValueRepresentation. CCL_INVALIDMEMBERPATH The member parameter does not address a valid sub-value of the object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COSignal.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclValueSetEnum(cclValueID valueID, int64_t x);
description: Description Sets a communication object value of Enumerated type. The integer representation matches a predefined enumeration of the API (e.g. cclServiceStateProvider, cclServiceStateConsumer, cclProvidedConnectionState, cclSubscriptionState, cclAnswerMode, cclCallState, etc.).
parameters: Parameters valueID ID of the value, returned by cclValueGetID cclCallContextValueGetID cclConsumedServiceValueGetID cclProvidedServiceValueGetID cclPDUSenderSignalValueGetID cclPDUReceiverSignalValueGetID cclPDUProviderSignalValueGetID x The value to be assigned.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The value was read out successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDVALUEID The valueID parameter does not identify a known value object. CCL_WRONGTYPE The value is no Enumerated type. CCL_VALUEREADONLY The value cannot be modified (e.g. a signal receiver port value).
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COSignal.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclValueSetHandler
syntax: Function Syntax typedef void(*cclValueHandler)(cclTime time, cclValueID valueID); int32_t cclValueSetHandler(cclValueID valueID, bool onUpdate, cclValueHandler handler);
description: Description Registers an event handler for a communication object value. Depending on the onUpdate flag the handler is called each time the value is updated or modified.
parameters: Parameters valueID ID of the value, returned by cclValueGetID cclCallContextValueGetID cclConsumedServiceValueGetID cclProvidedServiceValueGetID cclPDUSenderSignalValueGetID cclPDUReceiverSignalValueGetID cclPDUProviderSignalValueGetID onUpdate Controls whether the handler is called upon every value update or only when the value actually changes. handler The event routine to be called when the value is updated or modified. The event routine receives the current time of the measurement and the ID of the communication object value. You can specify the literal nullptr to remove a previously registered handler from the value.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The value was read out successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDVALUEID The valueID parameter does not identify a known value object.
example: Example The example source code is contained in file COSignal.c (Sample Configurations\Programming\C_Library\Examples).

name: cclValueSetString
syntax: Function Syntax int32_t cclValueSetString(cclValueID valueID, const char* str);
description: Description Sets a communication object value of String type.
parameters: Parameters valueID ID of the value, returned by cclValueGetID cclCallContextValueGetID cclConsumedServiceValueGetID cclProvidedServiceValueGetID cclPDUSenderSignalValueGetID cclPDUReceiverSignalValueGetID cclPDUProviderSignalValueGetID str The value to be assigned. The string is expected to be in UTF-8 encoding and zero-terminated. If the value is stored in a different encoding, the parameter is converted to that encoding before being stored.
returnValues: Return Values CCL_SUCCESS The value was read out successfully. CCL_WRONGSTATE The function may only be called during a running measurement, in the PreStartHandler of the measurement and in the StopHandler of the measurement. CCL_INVALIDVALUEID The valueID parameter does not identify a known value object. CCL_WRONGTYPE The value is no String type. CCL_VALUEREADONLY The value cannot be modified (e.g. a signal receiver port value).
